title: ops-light-secrets-server v0.1 — project charter & root
description:
# Charter — ops-light secrets server v0.1

**Objective.** Build an ops-light, self-hosted secrets server in Rust — one binary, no platform team — implementing a published subset of the Vault KV v2 + auth wire protocol so pinned, UNMODIFIED fnox / `vault` CLI / `bao` CLI versions work against it by configuration only. Open source, MIT, whole tree, no held-back tier (R19/KTD13). Also a professional-development project: the operator wants to genuinely understand sealing, identity, audit chaining, and envelope encryption; the primary success criterion is a written explainer produced without consulting the implementation, read back by a second person.

**Problem.** The pain is rotation, not storage: shared SaaS API keys (Populi/Canvas at a small non-profit) cannot be rotated because nobody can enumerate consumers (research: 64% of secrets exposed in 2022 were still active). Vault/OpenBao solve it with an ops surface (unseal ceremony, HA, policy DSL) that dwarfs the problem. Current best case: age-encrypted fnox.toml in git — safe on disk, not shareable, not rotatable without a commit.

**Session-settled key decisions (do not relitigate):**
1. Ops-light IS the product. The refusals (no HA, no clustering, no unseal ceremony, no policy language) are the wedge; the single-node ceiling is positioning.
2. Compose proven crates (axum, redb, age, chacha20poly1305, blake3, ed25519-dalek, rustls, clap, zeroize/secrecy). No crate ships a Vault KV v2 surface, an identity/token model, or a tamper-evident audit chain — that assembly is where the learning lands.
3. age recipient model over hand-built KEK/DEK: server holds an age identity supplied at boot (no unseal ceremony); age wraps a compact fixed-purpose keyring (KTD3); records use XChaCha20-Poly1305 AEAD bound to logical context so ciphertext cannot be transplanted.
4. Vault-API-compatible first. fnox-native protocol is phase 2, gated on a use-case-first design discussion with jdx (fnox discussion #615 — answered: direct-HTTP rewrite of fnox's vault provider is welcome upstream). AI agents are phase 3; identity schema carries kind (human|workload) from day one so agents land without migration.
5. Management surface = bundled CLI over an owner-only local Unix socket plus offline subcommands. NEVER remotely reachable (R34). One process, one binary, two routers.
6. Complexity budget — hard rules for the first two releases: no server-side process execution; no server-initiated network calls; no remotely reachable management routes; no second authorization model; no policy/validation expression language; no durable job queue or general-purpose/in-daemon user-work scheduler; no client-process supervision; no new compatibility endpoints without U0/U11 evidence.

**Architecture.** Client →(TLS + X-Vault-Token)→ raw-target guard → canonical Resource parse → single transaction coordinator (bounded blocking storage executor owning one redb file) → authn (accessor + keyed blake3 MAC, constant time) → authz (segment-prefix grants over a closed capability enum) → KV/rotation/mgmt services → XChaCha20-Poly1305 records + MAC-authenticated clear records. Every mutation commits state+audit atomically; every read withholds its response until its audit entry commits (R26). Audit = blake3 hash chain over encrypted payloads, anchored by externally signed ed25519 checkpoints mirrored off host — the daemon never holds the signing key (KTD4).

**Design gates:** G0 client-contract freeze (U0) · G1 redb proof spike (U2) · G2 clear-record/crypto-encoding/audit-schema freeze (U2/U6) · G3 logical-backup-format freeze (U10). Storage-format fixtures, the final data-plane router, and public compatibility claims wait on their gates; U1 scaffolding/config/control-socket work does not.

**Milestones:** M0 contract freeze → M1 vertical slice (real fnox read E2E) → M2 recovery-ready alpha → M3 rotation-ready v0.1.

**Threat-model digest.** Protected in v0.1: offline theft of store/backup without the age identity (values, credential verifiers, audit payloads are ciphertext; DB alone can neither disclose nor validate/mint credentials); authz bypass via ambiguous path encodings; ciphertext transplant; offline clear-record edits/rollback (MACs + checkpointed state digest); crash atomicity; audit tamper through last retained checkpoint; restore-based credential resurrection (epochs); clock-rollback TTL extension bounded across crash/restart by the frozen high-water checkpoint interval plus tolerance, with no in-process regression; bounded per-request/admission work and storage amplification from unauthenticated traffic. Availability under an arbitrary network flood is NOT claimed. NOT protected: live root/debugger on the unlocked process; holder of store+boot key; client-side leaks after an authorized read; upstream SaaS compromise; RPO to last backup/checkpoint; structural-metadata disclosure (paths, identity names, grants, version numbers readable to whoever holds the DB — deliberate, documented); destroyed-version ciphertext surviving in old backups (destruction is logical, not cryptographic erasure).

**Deferred — do NOT build in v0.1:** KTD16 blind audit query index (its purpose key is minted at init but stays dormant); segmented audit retention/archive/prune (all events stay in the active DB; R27 capacity thresholds guard); Vault-CLI shim (BUSL exit ladder instead: (1) point fnox at `bao` CLI, (2) upstream direct-HTTP fnox provider, (3) documented `vault` install with honest licensing note); client-side discovery/import (v0.2); rotation campaigns beyond R33's status view + closeout guard; OIDC, metrics, operator-side rotation adapters, signed event outbox (v0.3, evidence-driven). Outside identity entirely: HA/clustering, unseal ceremony/KMS/TPM, policy DSL, web UI, hosted tier, dynamic secrets, plugins, in-daemon schedulers, plaintext bulk export, server-side secret-equality oracles.

**Supported v0.1 runtime:** Linux x86_64 + aarch64, host service (systemd reference deployment), local ext4/XFS with working advisory locks. Release claims apply only to the tested matrix (R38).

**Conventions binding every bead:** test-first on authorization, token-lifecycle, and audit-chain paths; all state flows through the transaction coordinator (module visibility enforces it); secrets never in argv/logs/traces/errors/panics (R25, R37); every acceptance example (AE1–AE13) lands as an automated test; fail-closed outranks throughput at every margin. Test observability: every integration/e2e suite logs scenario begin/end, each step, and failure context through the shared harness (U11.9a, olss-charter-qul.12.10); detailed logs are mandatory AND canary-scanned — logging never becomes the leak (R25).

Source plan (provenance; beads are the execution source of truth): docs/plans/2026-07-16-002-feat-ops-light-secrets-server-plan.md. Naming: `ops-light-secrets-server` is the current candidate; OQ3 must finalize the authoritative naming tuple by an explicit retain-or-rename decision before ANY public artifact. Retaining it is a valid final choice.
**Plan-review corrections (binding):** the professional-development criterion is owned by U12.7 (olss-charter-qul.13.8); M3/root cannot close without the operator-authored first draft, second-reader record, and corrected revision. Across crash/restart, system time alone cannot prove zero TTL extension: U1.5 therefore persists a coalesced idle high-water checkpoint and freezes the honest maximum rollback window (checkpoint interval + tolerance + commit latency); effective time still never regresses within a live process. A single-purpose watermark safety loop is an invariant monitor, not the forbidden general-purpose scheduler. Every failure artifact is canary-scanned BEFORE retention/upload on an unconditional cleanup path; a hit quarantines raw artifacts and publishes only a sanitized diagnostic manifest.

**Final plan-audit invariants (binding):** every code-bearing bead owns named unit cases for its pure logic, boundaries, malformed/error paths, and state transitions; integration/fault/E2E tests prove composition rather than substituting for unit coverage. U11.9a provides the typed unit/integration event contract and U11.8 validates the executed-case manifest for every numbered scenario. Raw child/test output is private until an in-job canary decision; CI renders only post-scan typed summaries or bounded clean tails.

In default operation and every production-supported shipped workflow, raw bootstrap/emergency credentials and workload secret_ids are never deliberately persisted to a regular/block-device plaintext sink, argv, environment, logs, or artifacts. Approved disclosure is TTY, pipe/socket, or anonymous memory-backed FD with a precommit acknowledgement protocol; durable custody is delegated through that channel directly into operator encryption/password-manager tooling. Approved systemd boot delivery uses runtime/tmpfs credentials, with encrypted-at-rest provisioning for any persistent source. Environment delivery remains an explicitly unsafe, development-only compatibility escape hatch: it is off by default, conspicuously warned, excluded from systemd/docs/release acceptance, and tested to refuse without the unsafe flag.

**Transport claim boundary:** shipped production/F1/release workflows use TLS. Plaintext is refused on every non-loopback bind. Plaintext loopback TCP remains an explicit same-host development/proxy trust exception—not encrypted transport—and the Unix backend is recommended on multi-user hosts; public claims say “never over a non-loopback network,” not the false absolute “never on any network interface.”

**Operational completeness added by plan review:** v0.1 includes explicit no-auto-start forward store migration, offline compaction without erasure claims, fresh signing-trust enrollment and bounded old-key-authorized rollover, state-bound signed+DR-rehearsed backup prerequisites for destructive whole-store jobs, exact pending-anchor recovery, canonical recipient-set management, and one generated/tested CLI grammar. These are release requirements with unit/golden/fault/E2E coverage, not post-v0.1 documentation promises.

**Raw-artifact quarantine meaning:** in CI, quarantine is same-job inaccessible mode-0700 staging only until sanitized metadata is emitted, followed by guaranteed teardown/destruction; it is never uploaded, cached, attached, or retained across jobs. A scan hit, scanner error, or teardown failure fails the job and withholds every raw byte; only allowlisted sanitized metadata may render. Any local developer retention is explicit opt-in, outside the repo/CI artifact tree, mode-0700, TTL-bounded, loudly warned, and never accepted as release evidence. Tests force scan hit and teardown failure and prove upload/cache hooks receive nothing.

**Operability invariant added by final plan audit:** every persisted crash/reply-loss state has a named least-privilege discovery and recovery command. Backup and audit-export owners get list/show/resume without broad diagnostics; recovery.reserve gets status/release/recreate without direct filesystem/database surgery. Destructive authority changes carry immutable targets, generation where applicable, and non-secret audited reasons. Rotation machine truth distinguishes immutable target adoption from whether that version remains current. All of these contracts require pure unit tests plus integration/fault and assembled E2E evidence through the shared typed logging/canary pipeline.
close_reason: Root charter complete: M1/M2/M3 closed; remaining open leaves none.

title: U0: Client characterization & compatibility contract capture
description:
**Goal:** Replace assumptions about fnox and Vault/OpenBao client behavior with reproducible recorded evidence from pinned versions, BEFORE the API router is finalized. This unit is the evidence base for the entire compatibility story (R1 endpoint matrix, R2 config-only guarantee, KTD14 pinned matrix) and for the BUSL exit ladder.

**Why it exists:** fnox's Vault provider currently shells out to the BUSL-1.1-licensed `vault` CLI (`Command::new("vault")` running `vault kv get -field=<field> <path>`, failing with an install hint when absent) — but the provider surface has grown configuration (`address`, `token`, `credential_command`) that may change transport across releases. So the compatibility target is recorded behavior of pinned versions, never folklore. The mount-discovery preflight (`sys/internal/ui/mounts/<path>`) is a Vault-internal endpoint and the historically fragile compat joint — capture it exactly. R15's single-binary claim covers the server, not its clients: whether a consumer host needs the BUSL `vault` binary is one of this unit's named questions.

**Scope (children):** pin the matrix, capture pinned-client behavior against a recording stub, normalize + commit fixtures, draft docs/compatibility.md, close Gate G0.

**Done-when:** the named questions are answered with committed evidence — (a) which endpoints each pinned client actually hits (preflight? seal-status? health? lookup-self?), (b) how tokens and TLS roots are supplied, (c) whether a fresh host runs fnox without the BUSL `vault` binary, (d) what path-segment characters each pinned client emits and how they are encoded on the wire — NOT when the harness is beautiful.

**Files:** research/compat/, tests/fixtures/client-traces/, docs/compatibility.md (initial draft).

**Sequencing:** no dependencies; runs in parallel with U1. Gates the FINAL data-plane router (U5) and public compatibility claims (G0) — it does not gate scaffolding, configuration, local init design, or the control socket.

**Verification:** fixtures committed; matrix reviewed; R1/AE8 updated where captured behavior contradicts plan assumptions.
notes: Epic verification (2026-07-16): all five scoped U0 descendants are closed with dedicated pushed completion commits. The retained six-rung fixtures answer endpoints, VAULT_TOKEN/VAULT_CACERT supply, fnox subprocess behavior, Bao-with-vault-name non-BUSL onboarding, path bytes/encoding, faults, and the explicit LIST evidence gap. R1, AE8, U5, and U11 affected contracts were updated. ./scripts/check-compat-pins.sh and ./scripts/check-compat-capture.sh pass; generated compatibility doc gate reports full-artifact-v1 clean typed provenance. Full local 13-check run 0d979d73681a3dffc01abb9312222a90 passed. No automatic CI was triggered.

title: U1: Scaffold, config, local init, control socket, fail-closed startup
description:
**Goal:** A single binary whose `init` creates a valid store locally and exactly once, whose `serve` loads and validates configuration and refuses to start in ANY unsafe configuration, and whose management surface is an owner-only local Unix control socket. Plus the clock model and graceful-shutdown skeleton.

**Requirements:** R15 (single binary, no cluster/external DB/platform team); R16 (task-oriented CLI, human + stable JSON output); R17 (first management credential: disclosure-before-commit ordering); R18 (fail-closed startup refusal matrix — every refusal exits non-zero naming the setting, validation completes before the listener binds); R30 (initialization local, exclusive, one-time); R34 (management only via owner-restricted local socket with SO_PEERCRED, or offline commands); R35 (serve requires lifecycle=ready and known schema version); R36 (graceful shutdown: stop admission, drain, flush, final unsigned chain-head/shutdown event, zeroize); R37 (secret values via stdin/FD/credentials-dir/hidden TTY prompt — never argv; env dev-only behind an explicit unsafe flag); R6 (data-dir, single-node store); KTD1 (two axum routers, one process); KTD2 caveat (redb's internal lock is silently skipped on no-lock filesystems → startup takes its own explicit OS-level exclusive lock via a lockfile and refuses when it cannot be acquired).

**Approach:** `clap` CLI (`init`, `serve`, later subcommands); config from environment + file with unknown keys REJECTED; secret values accepted only through typed secret-source descriptors. `init` takes the exclusive lock, refuses non-empty/already-initialized directories, creates store id, schema version, keyring, audit genesis, and the first management identity in ONE transaction — in R17's failure-safe order: validate credential output sink → generate → write+flush credential to sink → only then commit. (Keyring/audit-genesis/first-identity internals are wired in as U2/U6/U3/U4 land — this unit owns the command, ordering contract, locking, and refusal behavior.) `serve` validation runs before bind: key material present and keyring decryptable, store initialized, schema known and lifecycle=ready, safe data-dir ownership/permissions/symlinks, exclusive lock, wall clock vs persisted high-water mark within tolerance, no non-loopback listener without TLS.

**Execution note:** test-first on the refusal matrix — each unsafe configuration is a failing test before the check exists.

**Files:** src/main.rs, src/config.rs, src/startup.rs, src/control/mod.rs, src/cli.rs, tests/startup.rs.

**Verification:** `cargo test --test startup` green; manual `--help` shows documented settings.

**Sequencing:** no dependencies; starts in parallel with U0.

title: U2: Keyring and encrypted single-node store
description:
**Goal:** A versioned key-value store in ONE redb file, persisting only AEAD ciphertext for secret values under keys held in an age-wrapped fixed-purpose keyring, with first-class per-secret metadata and format/lifecycle stamps.

**Requirements:** R4 (encryption at rest under purpose-separated keys; every ciphertext authenticated against immutable logical context — store, record type, path, version, key id — so transplanted ciphertext fails; structural metadata clear-but-MAC-authenticated); R5/KTD7 (plaintext only in bounded zeroize-on-drop buffers; allocator/kernel/TLS copies documented out of scope); R6 (single-node, no external DB); R11 (versioned writes, storage side); R21 (the application never persists the age identity in plaintext config/store/argv/history; credentials-directory delivery is runtime/tmpfs, and any persistent systemd provisioning uses LoadCredentialEncrypted or equivalent encrypted-at-rest custody); R35 (format_version + lifecycle stamps); R39/KTD15 (keyed MACs on every security-relevant clear record; MAC failure ⇒ readiness false, never warning-only); KTD2 (redb, proof spike, explicit fd-lock); KTD3 (keyring + XChaCha20-Poly1305 + canonical binary encoding + fresh-nonce discipline); KTD11 (secret_meta first-class record).

**Opens with the KTD2 proof spike = Gate G1** (child bead). SQLite fallback triggers there or never — before higher units are shaped around the store.

**Tables:** `meta` (store id, format_version, lifecycle per R35, wall-clock high-water mark); `system_keyring` (the age envelope as ONE opaque value — not a sidecar file, so keyring changes commit atomically with their audit events); `secret_meta` (KTD11); `secrets` (versioned records: version number, created_time, key id, nonce, ciphertext). Later units add identities/grants/credentials/rotation/audit tables through the same schema module.

**Boot behavior:** server age identity loads into `secrecy::SecretBox`; the `system_keyring` envelope is read and decrypted exactly once; its embedded store id is compared with the clear store id BEFORE any other record is accepted — a transplanted envelope is rejected before readiness.

**Files:** src/store/mod.rs, src/store/crypto.rs, src/store/keyring.rs, tests/store.rs.

**Verification:** `cargo test --test store` green; hexdump spot-check shows ciphertext; spike results recorded against KTD2's quantitative gate.

**Depends:** U1 (scaffold). Feeds Gate G2 via checked-in crypto fixtures/vectors.

**Lifecycle ownership handoff:** U2 freezes/validates format and the closed lifecycle/marker codecs; it does not leave `migrating`/`compacting` as aspirational stamps. U10.7 owns no-auto-start forward migration and U10.8 owns offline active-file compaction, each consuming the U2 registry/MAC/AEAD invariants and preserving anchored history. U1.3 owns startup refusal/reconciliation dispatch.
notes: Review note (2026-07-17): U2 epic acceptance inherits U2.1's supported-target XChaCha measurement requirement, but this host is x86_64 and the user explicitly stated ARM64 testing is unavailable. Resolution: accept the measured redb Gate G1, x86_64 crypto result, committed native-only ARM measurement/verifier tooling, and all correctness/format tests; make no ARM64 performance claim. Verification: cargo test --test store 14/14; scripts/check-crypto-vectors.sh 3/3 plus byte-identical x86_64 vectors; all eight U2 children closed; final repository gauntlet a30e93f1c3134b5dd54ccf6e63ae2826 green. Residual risk is unmeasured native ARM64 cipher capacity.

title: U6: Transaction coordinator and tamper-evident audit
description:
**Goal:** THE single commit boundary through which every state change and audit entry flows atomically, plus a hash-chained confidential audit log with externally signed, registered checkpoints and an operable public signing-trust lifecycle from fresh enrollment through bounded old-key-authorized rollover — never records secret material, never gives the daemon private signing keys, and fails closed on write failure.

**Requirements:** R13 (one canonical, schema-versioned audit event per externally visible operation — event id, request id, audit schema version, identity, credential accessor, optional consumer-instance id, auth method, canonical resource, requested capability, operation, outcome, reason code, effective time, wall-clock observation, served/written version; NEVER values or credential material; flood drops aggregate into bounded entries); R14 (two-tier tamper evidence: local chain verification catches edit/remove/insert/reorder; verification against an independently retained signed checkpoint additionally catches wholesale re-chaining and truncation; live-forgery-before-anchor is the documented exclusion); R23 (observed-consumer queries scan primary events over a documented lookback window; v0.1 retains ALL events); R25 (secrets/credentials appear in NO server output — logs, traces, metrics labels, errors, panics; secret paths and identity names additionally banned from metrics labels); R26 (state+audit commit atomically; a read's response is withheld until its audit entry commits; audit-commit failure ⇒ client gets an error, no secret bytes); R27 capacity (warning + stop-admitting thresholds; preallocated recovery.reserve); R28 (admin changes linearized through the transaction boundary); KTD4 (blake3 per-entry chain over ENCRYPTED payloads; plaintext envelope = exactly {envelope_version, audit_epoch, epoch_sequence, effective_timestamp, previous_hash, ciphertext_digest} per U6.3's frozen encoding — chain verifies without decrypting; ed25519 checkpoints signed EXTERNALLY, daemon never holds the private key, no in-daemon scheduler; stateless key generation, one-time public trust enrollment, old-key-authorized transition, bounded retained public lineage, and historical checkpoint/manifest verification); KTD8 (one durability domain; one bounded blocking storage executor with a data lane + reserved control/recovery lane; explicit cancellation semantics; module visibility makes the coordinator the only commit path).

**Sequencing:** lands BEFORE U5 handlers are considered done — KTD8 makes audit a storage concern, not a late add-on. KTD16's index and segmented retention are deferred post-v0.1: the primary-event scan is the only query path and is always authoritative.

**Accepted cost (do not "optimize" away):** every audited operation, reads included, is a durable write serialized on redb's single writer. That is the single-node ceiling, named so nobody fixes the fail-closed design into a fast broken one. Batching is permissible later only if it still fails closed before the response.

**Files:** src/txn.rs, src/audit/mod.rs, src/audit/chain.rs, src/audit/checkpoint.rs, src/audit/trust.rs, src/cli.rs, tests/audit.rs, tests/fault/txn_crash.rs.

**Verification:** `cargo test --test audit --test txn_crash` green; a fresh store generates/enrolls trust, prepares/signs/registers a checkpoint, rolls to a new signer through the externally signed transition, verifies historical and new checkpoints/manifests, and rejects wrong-domain/fork/replay/limit cases; `<binary> audit verify` detects a hand-tampered log.

**Depends:** U2.
**Plan-review unit summary:** U6 includes capability-gated audit query/export, bounded internal MVCC readers, versioned privacy-minimal envelopes, clock watermark events, real reserve allocation/reconciliation and clean/forced shutdown integration. `tests/audit.rs`, txn_crash and U11.4a own detailed secret-safe evidence.

**Operational surface completion:** this unit includes the authenticated `store reserve status|release|recreate` commands and their online reserved-lane/offline-lock recovery modes. U6 is not complete merely because capacity arithmetic and marker codecs exist: healthy release must refuse, released state must remain non-ready, restart/offline recreation must work, and the unit/fault suites must prove every cross-domain state with detailed canary-safe logs.
notes: needs-user-review — epic acceptance resolution/evidence (2026-07-17):\nAll nine scoped U6 descendants are closed and their focused acceptance checks pass. U6.7 carries the detailed conflict: a public-key-only verifier cannot inspect evidence placed exclusively inside an age-encrypted container, and the current binary lacks the durable serve/control dispatcher needed to claim real store-mutating audit query/export CLI transactions. Safe resolution preserves the charter's confidentiality/durability invariants: public verification authenticates header+ciphertext; age recipients validate inner bindings; domain APIs and exact command capability mappings are complete for later durable assembly, with no fake in-memory command path.\nEpic verification: cargo test --test audit --test txn_crash --test checkpoint --test signing_trust --test capacity --test storage_executor --test transaction_coordinator --test control_mgmt => 73/73 passed. Immediately preceding full local CI-equivalent run ./scripts/verify.sh all with FNOX_E2E_RUN=1 and pinned Vault/Bao/fnox binaries passed native all-target, docs, Rust 1.85 MSRV build/all-target tests, harness clippy/native/MSRV tests, compatibility pin/capture/real-client checks, crypto vectors, and canary gate. scripts/check-naming.sh passed. br dep cycles reports none. ARM64 runtime verification remains externally unavailable per user.

title: U3: Identity, capability, resource, and token model
description:
**Goal:** Distinct typed identities, canonical-resource authorization with segment-prefix grants over a fixed capability set governing EVERY KV path form, and TTL-bearing, revocable, capability-thin tokens.

**This is the authorization boundary — an authz bug is a full-store disclosure.** Test-first; property tests mandatory. age recipients never enter this path: R8 scoping is a server-side check (Product Contract).

**Requirements:** R7 (every human/app/integration is a distinct identity carrying kind: human|workload, extensible — display/audit only in v0.1, no kind-specific enforcement); R8 (one canonical resource = configured mount + validated logical segments, constructed exactly once per request; every API form — data, metadata, list, delete, undelete, destroy, versioned read — converts to it BEFORE the grant check; parser REJECTS rather than normalizes: encoded separators, dot segments, empty middle segments, repeated separators, control chars, invalid UTF-8, double-decoding; grants are exact-path or segment-prefix subtree matches — no globs, no regex, no deny rules, no policy language); R9 (TTL + revocation before expiry); R22 (closed, versioned capability set: read-current, read-history, list, write, soft-delete/undelete, destroy, destroy-all[local-only]; management capabilities each distinct: identity/grant mgmt, credential issue/revoke, rotation mgmt, consumer enumeration, audit read/export, backup, key rotation, diagnostics; built-in bundles like admin/auditor are aliases, not a policy language); R28 (disable/reduction/revocation linearized through the storage transaction boundary — tokens are capability-thin: identity, expiry, revocation state, NEVER a grant snapshot; every authorized request reloads current grants, which is what makes R28 implementable without an invalidation bus; every resulting rejection audited); KTD5 (thin tokens); KTD9 (RawTargetGuard before route extraction — axum's Path extractor percent-decodes before handlers run, so validation after extraction cannot tell a literal separator from an encoded alias); KTD10 (matcher returns {allow, resource, operation, matched_grant | deny_reason} — hot path uses allow only; full decision feeds audit and a management-gated `authz explain`; never echoed to unauthorized clients).

**Files:** src/identity/mod.rs, src/authz/resource.rs, src/authz/grant.rs, src/identity/token.rs, tests/identity.rs.

**Verification:** `cargo test --test identity` green, property tests included.

**Depends:** U2 (MAC-authenticated records), U6 (linearization runs through the coordinator).

title: U4: Vault-compatible auth surface
description:
**Goal:** Unmodified Vault clients obtain and present tokens via token auth (X-Vault-Token), the AppRole login endpoint, and lookup-self — behind request-rate limits — with NO bootstrap route anywhere on the network (Flow F1 is U1's local `init`).

**Requirements:** R31 (the ENTIRE supported auth surface: X-Vault-Token, POST /v1/auth/approle/login, GET /v1/auth/token/lookup-self; v0.1 tokens non-renewable — login advertises renewable:false, renew-self returns R3's explicit unsupported error, workloads re-login via AppRole; AppRole secret_ids and their tokens carry audience `data`; the Vault listener accepts only data-audience tokens, the control socket only control-audience credentials); R27 (request-size + attempt-rate limits with bounded limiter state on the unauthenticated surface — this router is where it lives); R9 (TTL/revocation); R24 (raw credentials disclosed once at creation, never persisted/logged/recoverable; public accessor selects exactly one record; secret validated against a keyed non-recoverable verifier under a keyring-held key — DB alone cannot verify guesses or mint credentials; verifier domain includes store id, kind, audience, accessor, issue epoch); KTD5 (structured opaque credentials, keyed blake3 MAC, constant time, dummy verifier for unknown accessors, NO password KDF anywhere — Argon2 deliberately dropped: server-generated 256-bit secrets are full-entropy, and a KDF on the per-request token path would be a CPU-burn DoS amplifier); Flow F3 (workload boots, presents role_id+secret_id through an approved runtime channel, gets a TTL token, and reads scoped paths — no plaintext static credential on persistent disk).

**Execution note:** start from a failing test asserting the AppRole login response envelope byte-shape against U0's captured fixtures.

**Files:** src/auth/token.rs, src/auth/approle.rs, src/api/router.rs, tests/auth.rs.

**Verification:** `cargo test --test auth` green; envelope shape asserted against captured Vault responses.

**Depends:** U3.
**Plan-review unit summary:** U4 includes the concrete control-plane credential commands and `tests/control_mgmt.rs`; role deletion, orphan discovery/revocation, pagination, limiter isolation and detailed canary-clean harness logs are release requirements.

title: U5: Vault KV v2 API surface + vertical slice (M1)
description:
**Goal:** Serve the declared Vault KV v2 endpoint matrix — read, write with CAS, list, soft-delete/undelete/destroy, versioned read, metadata (custom_metadata, max_versions, cas_required), mount-discovery preflight, seal-status, health — so an unmodified `vault` CLI uses the server as a backend. Closes with the first product milestone (M1): a REAL pinned fnox read end-to-end over TLS.

**Requirements:** R1 (the generated method/path/status/body matrix in docs/compatibility.md is the normative artifact; prose never adds endpoints absent from contract tests); R3 (everything outside the declared surface — PATCH included — returns an explicit unsupported error naming the endpoint; never empty success, never silent approximation; DELETE /v1/:mount/metadata/:path is in the unsupported set — full purge is local-only per R29); R11 (cas=0 create-only; positive cas must equal current version; cas_required rejects unconditional writes); R29 (soft-delete reversible ≠ destroy; destroy removes a version's ciphertext from every read path — logical destruction, never a cryptographic-erasure claim; max_versions bounds history but NEVER retires the protected prior version of an incomplete rotation → retention_deferred_by_rotation); R27 (bounds: a secret version is ONE bounded encrypted JSON object — total bytes, field count, name length, nesting depth, list cardinality); Flow F2 (read authorized → durable audit commit → value returned; plaintext never intentionally persisted).

**Known fragile joint:** `sys/internal/ui/mounts/<path>` must return options.version:"2" exactly — assert against U0 fixtures. Error envelopes {"errors":[...]}, status codes, and field-presence rules are GENERATED from the captured/differential contract (U0/U11) — no hard-coded 404/empty-list folklore.

**KTD12:** default mount `secret/`, reported KV v2; one mount in v0.1; other mounts or nonempty Vault namespaces fail explicitly. KTD6: secret-bearing responses carry Cache-Control: no-store and are never compressed.

**Files:** src/api/kv.rs, src/api/sys.rs, src/api/errors.rs, tests/api_kv.rs, tests/vertical_slice.rs.

**Verification:** `cargo test --test api_kv --test vertical_slice` green; U11 exercises the real CLIs.

**Depends:** U0 (captured contract, Gate G0), U2, U4, U6 (every handler commits through the coordinator).
**Plan-review verification summary:** include local `secret purge`/remote-metadata-delete refusal and `tests/control_mgmt.rs` alongside api_kv/vertical_slice; U5.7 uses actual command-driven identity/credential setup and pinned fnox evidence.
notes: needs-user-review — epic acceptance resolution/evidence (2026-07-17):\nAll eight U5 descendants are closed. Conflict inherited from U5.7/U5.8: the durable assembled serve/control binary is still explicitly integration_pending, so real pinned client evidence runs against the production TLS/Auth/KV router rather than a long-running assembled daemon; secret purge parser/domain exist but live control dispatch awaits the common adapter. This is not represented as assembled-binary E2E.\nVerification: FNOX_E2E_RUN=1 with pinned archive/binary directories cargo test --test api_kv --test vertical_slice --test control_mgmt --test sys_api --test compat => 41/41 passed. Actual Vault 2.0.3, Bao 2.6.0, fnox 1.29.0 and 1.30.0 drove real rustls KV routes with checksum verification, hermetic state, network confinement, and canary scan. CAS, bounds, delete/undelete/destroy/metadata, unsupported surface, sys probes/mounts, local purge, remote refusal, audit-before-return and no-store behavior pass. Full local CI-equivalent gate passed immediately before U5.8; subsequent affected suites and all-target clippy pass. ARM64 remains externally unavailable per user.

title: U9: TLS transport, limits, and plaintext refusal
description:
**Goal:** Serve over rustls TLS with certificates reloadable WITHOUT restart (a restart costs an attended key ceremony — R36), bounded requests and timeouts, refusal of plaintext remote connections, reverse-proxy as an explicit listener type, and graceful-shutdown wiring.

**Requirements:** R20 (credentials/secrets never traverse a non-loopback network in cleartext; server refuses plaintext on every non-loopback bind; plaintext loopback TCP is an explicit same-host trust exception, with a Unix backend recommended on multi-user hosts; reverse proxy supported only with loopback/Unix backend hop and explicit boundary config; forwarded identity headers ignored by default); R18 (non-loopback bind without TLS fails startup — seam laid in U1, TLS path + runtime refusal here; loopback 127.0.0.0/8 and ::1 may run plaintext); R27 (TLS accept/HTTP parser enforce handshake, partial-header, header byte/count, connection, and slow-body limits before Tower allocation; Tower enforces post-parse body/routing/concurrency/request/drain limits); R36 (TLS reload without restart; graceful shutdown: stop admission → drain bounded in-flight → flush → final unsigned shutdown event → zeroize); KTD6 (bearer tokens over rustls — exactly what unmodified Vault clients expect; no mTLS, no Tailscale-only posture; Cache-Control: no-store, no compression on secret-bearing responses); KTD1 adoption gate (`axum-server` community adapter is ADOPTED only after this unit proves TLS reload + graceful shutdown work — treat it as an independent dependency in review).

**Middleware ordering is security-significant and covered by an integration test:** raw-target guard → header-count/size bounds → sensitive-header marking → tracing/request-id → rate/concurrency limits (source-keyed) → authentication → per-identity rate limits (U4.3) → handler. X-Vault-Token (and any future credential-bearing header) is marked sensitive via tower-http BEFORE tracing is invoked (KTD9, R25).

**Reverse-proxy mode** is an explicit listener type, not a boolean relaxation: binds only a configured Unix socket or loopback address; forwarded source headers accepted only from the exact configured proxy peer in one allowlisted syntax — otherwise ignored; rate-limit source buckets use the verified proxy-derived source only in this mode.

**Files:** src/transport.rs, src/startup.rs, tests/transport.rs.

**Verification:** `cargo test --test transport` green.

**Depends:** U1.
**Plan-review final adapter/shutdown contract:** U9.1 records only provisional TLS/reload suitability; U9.4 proves the executor barrier, clean-vs-forced shutdown, readiness/TCP-liveness behavior and records final adapter adoption. The `tls reload` command uses the existing control authorization model, not a second one.
notes: needs-user-review — epic acceptance resolution/evidence (2026-07-17):\nAll four U9 descendants are closed. Conflict inherited from U9.4: the project has production axum-server acceptor composition, rustls reload primitive, exact TransportManage authorization, bounds, drain and shutdown barrier hooks, but the top-level durable daemon assembly remains integration_pending. Therefore tests prove the live transport components and real TLS requests, not an installed long-running systemd daemon.\nVerification: cargo test --test transport --test middleware_stack --test startup --test startup_contract --test startup_skeleton --test storage_executor => 33/33 passed. Covers TLS/plaintext refusal, in-flight reload, mismatch/audit rollback, crash reconciliation, exact authorization, connection/handshake limits, proxy/source behavior, security-significant middleware order, drain readiness/TCP behavior, executor shutdown cancellation/barrier, startup refusal and safe observability. axum-server 0.8.0 adoption remains pinned. Full local CI-equivalent native/MSRV/canary gate passed after U9.4. ARM64 runtime unavailable per user.

title: U7: Consumers and rotation surfaces
description:
**Goal:** The reason the product exists (Flow F4): maintain the declared-consumer registry, reconcile declared/authorized/observed consumer views, surface a secret's age against a rotation interval, run the begin → cutover → closeout rotation record, and show post-write version adoption — so a key is rotated with the consumer list known BEFORE the change, not discovered by an outage.

**Requirements:** R10 (consumer view reports two separately labeled facts never collapsed: AUTHORIZED = every identity whose current grants permit read; OBSERVED = audited reads with last-read time + version served, inside R23's window; recency ANNOTATES, never filters); R12 (secret age = time since last completed rotation, else creation; per-secret interval lives in server-owned rotation metadata writable ONLY via the local control plane — Vault custom_metadata stays client-owned and cannot alter rotation policy; values written outside a rotation are labeled changed_since_last_completed_rotation); R23 (documented lookback window; scan primary audit events — KTD16 index deferred); R29 (a rotation-protected prior version may exceed max_versions → retention_deferred_by_rotation; closeout/cancel/supersession re-evaluates pruning); R33 (post-write status per snapshotted consumer: on-current / on-prior / silent-since-write, from audited read versions at declared-consumer, identity, AND stable consumer-instance granularity — one identity's replicas can disagree; language is "fetched version N", never "using"; completion guarded: every snapshotted consumer on-current OR explicit --acknowledge-unverified with an audited reason; expired/revoked instances = retired-without-proof, need a recorded reason or the override; redacted closeout report rides the audit chain — no separate signature scheme; beyond the one guard, observation only — no auto-revocation, no campaign state machine); R40 (registry record: stable consumer id, canonical resource, owner, environment, source, optional identity id, optional instance id, lifecycle status, last-verified, non-secret note — NEVER a secret/command/script/webhook; reconciliation dimensions and closed flags: declared_unmigrated, declared_unauthorized, authorized_undeclared, migrated_unobserved, observed_undeclared, reconciled_observed, retired_historical; rotation snapshots = union of declared + authorized, observed as evidence; an unresolved declared consumer blocks closeout unless overridden).

**Rotation lifecycle (R11):** begin snapshots consumers + current version + expected CAS without writing; cutover atomically CAS-writes the target and transitions prepared→cutover (CAS conflict leaves it prepared — never a silently changed snapshot); states prepared | cutover | completed | cancelled-before-cutover | superseded; NO post-cutover abort — recovery is explicit copy-forward rollback to a new higher version; the current-version pointer never moves backward.

**Files:** src/consumer.rs, src/rotation.rs, src/api/kv.rs, tests/rotation.rs.

**Verification:** `cargo test --test rotation` green (AE3, AE11, AE13 scenarios).

**Depends:** U5, U6.

**Operator catalog:** exact consumer/instance CRUD-retire commands, `consumer reconcile`, immutable rotation ids, bounded rotation list/show/status history, and portfolio `rotation due|list` are first-class v0.1 surfaces. Completion treats consumer-evidence and upstream-not-revoked overrides as separate risky ceremonies; all original begin/cutover/rollback/retention functionality remains.
close_reason: U7 complete: rotation status/closeout/interval/suite leaves closed.

title: U8: Per-purpose key rotation — recipient, record, credential, metadata, audit
description:
**Goal:** An operator can rotate the server's age recipient cheaply (atomic keyring rewrap — no record touched), rotate the record-encryption key by an offline whole-store pass, and end every remotely usable credential with one local incident command (R41) — with NO reachable mixed or half-rotated state, ever.

**Requirements:** R21 (custody: active identity via approved boot channel, recovery copy off-host); R35 (record-key rotation runs under lifecycle=reencrypting; serve refuses to start during it); R39 (metadata-integrity key rotation = offline rewrite + MAC regeneration); R41 (`credential epoch rotate --reason <...>`: increments current_credential_epoch, generates a new credential-verifier key, invalidates every outstanding token and secret_id, mints a replacement emergency control credential using R17's disclosure-before-commit ordering — reuses R32's epoch primitive); KTD3 (age has NO rotation primitive — all rotations are explicit application-level jobs; fresh nonces on every re-encryption even for unchanged plaintext); KTD8 (recipient rewrap commits new envelope + incremented keyring generation + audit event in ONE redb transaction).

**Per-purpose-key rotation semantics — fixed here so no generic "rotate all keys" command can promise the impossible:**
- secret-record key: offline whole-store re-encryption (new store file, fsync, atomic rename; interrupted pass leaves the original authoritative with only its lifecycle stamp changed (U8.2) — delete partial .new and rerun; no resumable-cursor machinery);
- metadata-integrity key: offline rewrite + MAC regeneration;
- audit-payload key: FORWARD-ONLY, retaining decrypt-only historical keys — rewriting ciphertext would invalidate anchored audit hashes;
- credential-verifier key: only together with a credential-epoch increment (R41);
- audit-index key: minted-but-dormant (KTD16 deferred; rotates by index rebuild if the index ever ships).

Audit history survives every operation.

**Files:** src/store/reencrypt.rs, src/cli.rs, tests/reencrypt.rs, tests/key_recovery.rs.

**Verification:** `cargo test --test reencrypt` and `cargo test --test key_recovery` green (U8.4 runs both).

**Depends:** U2.
**Plan-review U8 decomposition:** U8.3 is now only credential-epoch/shared emergency access; U8.5 owns offline metadata-integrity re-MAC; U8.6 owns forward-only audit-payload generation rotation; U8.4a lands recovery-critical recipient/epoch tests before the full U8.4 suite. All five purpose-key semantics and every prior test remain release requirements, while restore/M2 no longer wait for record re-encryption.

**Final decomposition:** recipient rewrap (U8.1), record re-encrypt (U8.2), credential epoch/emergency access (U8.3), metadata re-MAC (U8.5), and audit-payload generation rotation (U8.6) are distinct operations with distinct authority, liveness, crash, and anchoring semantics. U8.4 runs every slice without merging them into a generic rotate-all command.
close_reason: U8 complete: recipient/epoch/record/metadata/audit key rotation leaves closed.

title: U10: Backup, restore, forward migration, and compaction
description:
**Goal:** A single operator can create/sign/rehearse an encrypted transactionally consistent backup of the RUNNING server, restore it safely on a fresh host, migrate a prior supported store forward, and compact obsolete active-file pages—each by a documented recovery-first sequence that cannot resurrect credentials, activate corrupt state, or leave an ambiguous file/lifecycle/anchor state.

**Requirements:** R32 (application-level logical archive from ONE consistent read transaction — never a copy of the live redb file as normative format; versioned canonical frames per table, encrypted record bytes unchanged, authenticated clear-record bytes, current keyring envelope, ALL audit events, per-table counts/digests, canonical manifest with store id, schema version, keyring generation, U2.5 non-audit state digest, chain head, latest included checkpoint and checksums, plus a detached signature over its domain-separated manifest+content digest; recovery keyring rewrapped inside the archive to the snapshot-derived effective backup set with fingerprints/generations recorded—fresh-host restore uses a matching recovery-recipient identity and a separately generated target-active identity, NOT the failed host's active identity; archive NEVER contains a private age identity or the checkpoint signing key — the documented recovery package pairs archive + offline keys, and a store backup without them is not a recovery; restore builds in a temp sibling, verifies every frame/MAC/ciphertext/audit relationship/schema/count, fsyncs, atomically installs, and increments current_credential_epoch so every pre-restore token/secret_id fails epoch validation; identities/grants/secrets/audit survive, bearer credentials are reissued — restore mints and discloses a replacement emergency control credential via R41's primitive (U8.3, R17 ordering), because the epoch bump kills every pre-restore credential including control ones and a restored store must never be unauthenticatable); R14 (chain verifies against the last off-host checkpoint); R35 (restore under lifecycle=restoring); KTD17 (backup format versioned independently of redb — release-fixture restores stay stable across redb file-format changes; rollback recovery = restoring behind the newest independently retained checkpoint: requires explicit --start-recovery-epoch + reason, increments credential AND audit epochs, opens the new epoch with a fork-record event — verification reports the historical anchored epoch and the recovery fork separately, never presenting the chain as continuous).

**`backup verify --full` is the standing disaster-recovery rehearsal:** restore into an isolated temp dir, validate schema/tables, authenticate and decrypt EVERY encrypted record without emitting plaintext, verify chain + checkpoint relationship, tear down, record the success so doctor reports rehearsal age. A backup that has never survived a rehearsal is treated as unproven, and the docs say so.

**Owns Gate G3:** the logical backup format + restore/audit-epoch rules freeze here (child bead) — one of M0's four gates.

**Files:** src/backup.rs, src/store/migrate.rs, src/store/compact.rs, src/cli.rs, docs/operating.md, tests/backup.rs, tests/migration.rs, tests/compaction.rs.

**Verification:** backup/restore, migration, and compaction unit/integration suites green; prior-release/synthetic migration compatibility, compaction logical preservation+size reduction, every fault boundary, detached trust-lineage verification, both offline rehearsals, and documented E2E sequences pass (AE7).

**Depends:** U2, U6.

**Maintenance ownership:** U10.7 owns no-auto-start forward migration and U10.8 owns non-erasure offline compaction. Both require authenticated plan/apply/abort, signed+recovery-rehearsed backup, capacity/reserve preflight, explicit cross-domain marker protocol, temp-sibling verification+atomic install, and a distinct persistent pending anchor cleared only by prepare→sign→register+state verify. They remain separate operations and tests; neither hides inside backup, restore, or key rotation.
notes: needs-user-review — epic acceptance resolution/evidence (2026-07-17):\nAll eight U10 descendants are closed. The recovery domain, archive, restore, rehearsal, migration, compaction and rollback-fork components are real and verified. The top-level running-daemon backup/control sequence and installed rollback-fork handoff remain unavailable until the common serve/control assembly; open scoped U11.9r is the explicit M2 assembled-binary evidence owner, so this epic does not claim that external proof.\nVerification: cargo test --test backup --test restore --test backup_verify --test recovery_fork --test migration --test compaction --test backup_format => 42/42 passed. Immediately preceding U10.6 matrix also passed backup_cli/signing trust for 35/35 and all-target clippy. Evidence covers logical single-snapshot archives, canonical registry/container/signatures, recovery-only restore, epoch/disclosure/target atomicity, rehearsal/receipts, rollback classification/fork marker, prior logical format, migration registry/history preservation and compaction recovery-first capacity/state preservation. 26 shared-harness recovery scenarios have source evidence and clean canary attestation. Full assembled-daemon AE7 remains U11.9r, not silently waived.

title: U11: Compatibility harness, acceptance, fault, fuzz, differential suites
description:
**Goal:** Every acceptance example (AE1–AE13) runs as an automated test; KV reads are proven against the REAL pinned `fnox`, `vault`, and `bao` binaries; the invariants the design leans on — crash atomicity, parser safety, no secret leakage — are attacked continuously, not assumed; a differential corpus keeps the server honest against a pinned OpenBao reference rather than its own fixtures alone.

**Requirements:** R1 (matrix fidelity), R2 (config-only compat, the actual fnox binary is the acceptance subject — not a stand-in CLI), R3, R25, R26; AE1–AE13; KTD14 (pins in CI so a client upgrade cannot silently break R2).

**Standing suites:**
- **compat harness:** starts a server instance, drives it with pinned fnox (primary client, first-class gate), `vault` CLI, and `bao` (the non-BUSL path); asserts reads/writes/lists/preflight succeed configuration-only.
- **differential:** replays a normalized request corpus (reads, writes, CAS conflicts, deletes/undeletes/destroys, metadata, lists, missing paths, auth errors) against BOTH a pinned OpenBao reference server and this implementation; compares status class, error envelope, field presence; volatile fields normalized; deliberate divergences (R3's unsupported surface) allowlisted WITH stated reasons.
- **acceptance:** AE1–AE13 as integration tests asserting product-level outcomes.
- **fault:** child-process kill points around write/revoke/backup/re-encrypt; disk-full and short-write injection (feeds AE9).
- **property:** canonical path/grant equivalence models (with U3).
- **fuzz:** token strings, request paths, JSON bodies, encrypted record + checkpoint decoders — scheduled in CI.
- **canary scan:** seeded secret values must appear in NO log, trace, panic, or test artifact (R25) — includes U0 fixtures.
- **benchmark:** audited-read/write baseline on a named reference host, durability settings stated; later changes cannot materially regress it without explicit review.
- **e2e gauntlet:** scripted from-scratch run of the assembled system — build/artifact select → safe key/trust bootstrap → init/serve → pinned clients → credential rollover → consumer/rotation matrix → checkpoint/trust rollover → signed backup/rehearsal/restore/fork → record/metadata/audit key jobs → signed audit export → prior-fixture migration → compaction → doctor — with per-stage detailed logging and a collected run directory (U11.9).

**Files:** tests/compat/mod.rs, tests/acceptance/mod.rs, tests/fault/, tests/differential/, fuzz/, benches/, scripts/e2e.sh, .github/workflows/ci.yml.

**Verification:** compat + acceptance + fault suites green in CI against pinned clients; fuzz corpus checked in.

**Depends:** U5, U6, U7. Runs continuously once U5 exists; complete only when every acceptance example passes.
**Plan-review suite architecture:** U11.0 supplies CI early; U11.9a supplies typed secret-safe logs; U11.4a supplies the core/recovery fault gate; U11.9r supplies recovery-alpha assembled-binary evidence; U11.4/U11.9 remain full M3 extensions. Source-full, recovery-alpha and artifact-smoke share one stage library/run manifest. Any failure runs cleanup+scan before raw artifact retention; verification manifest/ignore ledger finalization is U11.8.

**Final suite architecture:** U11.0 establishes the bootstrap runner; U11.9a adds typed unit/integration observability; U11.6 is the early comprehensive artifact gate. Per-unit suites, compatibility and differential tests, AE acceptance, both fuzz groups, benchmark, U11.4a core/recovery faults, U11.4 rotation/full-key/migration/compaction maintenance faults, recovery-alpha, source-full, and immutable artifact-smoke feed U11.8's global executed-case manifest. Recovery, doctor, CLI, systemd, and docs producers are dependencies where their checks are claimed; the stale shorthand “depends U5/U6/U7” is not the execution graph.

**Verification:** all raw outputs are private until same-job scan; typed results include exact commit/binary/client digests and safe timings/reproducers. Required PR checks plus exact-candidate fresh scheduled fuzz/performance evidence must be green; no ignored security scenario or silent profile skip remains at M3.

**Standing-suite additions:** the unit/component matrix covers reasoned deactivation and target/current rotation semantics; fault coverage owns reserve and publication interruption states; recovery-alpha/source-full/artifact-smoke execute reserve and owner-catalog commands; the global manifest maps every new command/state to its unit, fault, and E2E case. Detailed logs remain typed and scan-gated in the producing job.
close_reason: U11 complete: compat, differential, acceptance, fault, fuzz, e2e, verification contract closed.

title: U12: Operator experience — doctor, docs, licensing, release integrity
description:
**Goal:** A first-time operator reaches a first successful secret read by ONE documented sequence; recurring operations have coherent CLI workflows and a `doctor`; the project ships MIT-licensed with everything in the open tree and attestable release artifacts.

**Requirements:** R16 (CLI command families as a coherent surface: init, serve, doctor, identity, grant, token, approle, secret, consumer, rotation, authz explain, audit, key, credential epoch rotate, clock repair, backup, restore — each with human-readable AND stable JSON output); R17/R21/R37 (secret input never via argv; key-custody documentation); R19/KTD13 (MIT, entire tree, no ee/ carve-out — revised from MPL-2.0 at wayfinder T13, Robert's call; closed forks knowingly accepted; anti-BUSL posture expressed by openness, not copyleft); R38 (attestable releases: signed checksums, SBOM, build provenance; MSRV pinned; Cargo.lock committed + --locked enforced; cargo-deny licenses/advisories in CI; SECURITY.md reporting process; upgrade notes per storage-format change; release smoke downloads the immutable staged candidate through the release delivery path and post-promotion re-verifies the identical published digest including restoring a store fixture from at least one prior release; claims name supported OS/arch/filesystem/deployment — cross-compilation alone promotes nothing); Flow F1.

**README leads with the settled positioning paragraph** (using OQ3's authoritative final naming tuple): single-binary Vault-KV-v2-compatible secrets server for teams too small to run Vault; exists for one workflow — rotating a credential without an outage; every read audited atomically; declared/authorized/observed never collapsed; ops-light claim is a list of refusals; management on a local socket; works with unmodified vault/bao CLIs and fnox; MIT, whole tree. README also says "outgrow it → OpenBao" out loud.

**Files:** README.md, docs/operating.md, docs/compatibility.md, docs/cli.md (generated from --help, U12.6), src/cli.rs (doctor), LICENSE, CONTRIBUTING.md, SECURITY.md, deny.toml, examples/systemd/, .github/workflows/release.yml.

**Verification:** AE8 green; `cargo deny check` passes; release artifacts carry checksums, signatures, SBOM, provenance; a second reader can follow F1 unaided (Success Criteria).

**Depends:** U1–U11 (closes the release).
**Plan-review release/learning additions:** U12.7 (olss-charter-qul.13.8) owns the operator-authored explainer/second-reader gate. Release operations consume U11's recovery-alpha, source-full and immutable artifact-smoke profiles and publish artifacts only after unconditional canary safety. CLI contract tests and tested systemd units are explicit deliverables, not documentation assertions.

**Final files/verification:** keep the command inventory synchronized with U12.6, including TLS reload, checkpoint sign, backup manifest/rehearsal, purge, and split U8.5/U8.6 operations. Deliver release-trust policy, generated CLI/compat docs, hardened tested systemd backup+sign/checkpoint units, upgrade notes/fixture registry, and U12.7 learning evidence. A candidate cannot promote until U11.8, operating/compat docs, CLI contract, U12.7, native matrix smoke, and same-job artifact scans are green.
close_reason: U12 complete: doctor, docs, packaging, systemd, CLI coherence, release pipeline closed.

title: Gate G2: freeze clear-record format, canonical crypto encoding, audit event schema
description:
**Review-and-freeze decision gate.** Before persistent-format fixtures become binding, review and FREEZE the cross-release-verifiable formats together (they share the canonical-encoding discipline):

1. **Authenticated clear-record format (KTD15/R39):** fixed binary encoding + keyed MAC under the keyring's metadata-integrity key; MAC domain = store id, table id, primary key, schema version, record generation, canonical value bytes. Covers identities, grants, credential metadata, secret metadata (incl. the version-set summary — U2.2), rotation state, consumer declarations, schema metadata, the wall-clock high-water-mark record (U1.5), keyring metadata (U2.3's clear record).
2. **Canonical crypto encoding (KTD3):** record headers + AEAD associated data = magic bytes, format version, cipher_suite_id, store id, record-domain id, key id, length-prefixed mount and canonical path segments, logical record id, secret version where applicable, created_time. NO JSON, debug formatting, string concatenation, platform endianness, or map iteration order in any authenticated encoding.
3. **Audit event schema + chain/anchor encodings (R13/KTD4/KTD15):** canonical, schema-versioned event fields (event id, request id, schema version, identity, accessor, consumer-instance id, auth method, canonical resource, capability, operation, outcome, reason code, effective time, wall-clock observation, served/written version) + the plaintext chain envelope (audit-epoch id, epoch-scoped sequence, timestamp, previous hash, ciphertext digest — U6.3's KTD17-ready form) + the checkpoint canonical descriptor encoding (U6.4) + the checkpoint public-key descriptor/trust-transition lineage encoding with bounded count/size (U6.8) + the state-digest tuple encoding (U2.5). The last two are cross-release verifiable formats — R14 requires OLD checkpoint files to re-verify after upgrades — so they freeze here, not at some later gate.
4. **Recovery-family event types (KTD17):** the restore recovery event, the rollback fork-record event, and the emergency-credential-issuance record are defined JOINTLY with U10.1 (this gate depends on it) so the frozen schema includes them — G3 must not need a post-freeze schema change.

**Freeze means:** checked-in test vectors and cross-version fixtures become binding — specifically U2.7's header/AAD/MAC/keyring vectors, U6.3's audit-event encoding vectors + chain-envelope fixtures, U6.4's checkpoint-descriptor + state-digest vectors, and U6.8's key-descriptor/trust-transition/lineage vectors; any later change is an R35 forward-only schema migration shipping upgrade notes (R38). Explicitly NOT in this gate: KTD16's audit query-index format — deferred post-v0.1; the index is rebuildable from primary events, so its format is never load-bearing (wayfinder T08).

**Exit:** formats reviewed against the recorded designs, all named vector sets committed, sign-off noted here. Feeds milestone M0.
**Plan-review freeze boundary:** G2 freezes the common authenticated-clear-record envelope/domain registry and only concrete value codecs that exist at M0. Future identity/credential/rotation/consumer domains are not fictionally frozen early; before each first durable write, its owner registers a unique id/version and lands golden/negative edit/transplant/unknown/trailing vectors under the frozen registry. Freeze audit `envelope_version` while keeping operation/type encrypted.

The freeze manifest lists every format/domain id, exact field widths/order/endianness/time unit/length bounds, MAC/AEAD/hash domain, unknown-version policy, vector hashes/generator/test commands, recovery-family handoff owner and review sign-off. Candidate vectors may exist before gate close; after close they are binding and change only by forward migration.

**Final format-registry verification:** freeze the audit mutation-delta and bulk-transition codecs—including pre-registered store-migration, store-compaction, lifecycle-marker, pending-anchor, and anchor-clear operation ids—plus the audit-event AEAD domain/frame and authoritative backup table/domain ids alongside existing formats. A linter fails duplicate/reserved ids, missing owner/codec/vector, cross-domain reuse, or a durable table absent from backup coverage. Vector generation is reproducible and byte-drift reviewed; mutate every field/domain and run positive/negative decode on x86_64 plus native/emulated aarch64. All generator/test output passes U11.6 before retention.

**Signer-eligibility freeze:** checkpoint, backup-manifest, audit-export, and receipt domains encode creator epoch/sequence/head where server-created, trust-lineage generation/transition digest, expected current signer id, and domain. Registration is accepted only while that key is current; rollover requires outstanding descriptors registered or audited abandoned. Vectors reject retired keys on registration/new artifacts, wrong interval/generation/transition/domain, and attempts to complete old unsigned artifacts after rollover. The format does not claim a cryptographic signing timestamp.

**Maintenance-preflight allowlist codec:** freeze the versioned closed operation/state-domain allowlist used to reverse-replay S+1..H: backup output/signature/receipt/checkpoint bookkeeping; audit-only read/verification with no protected-state delta; U1.5 clock-watermark operational delta; expected clean-shutdown event/marker. Unknown or substantive domains and forced/unclean shutdown refuse. Vectors reconstruct `state_digest_at_S`, mutate each allowed/forbidden delta, and bind S/H/tail digest.

**Signing-transition construction/boundary vectors:** freeze the realizable non-cyclic order: commit a prepare-intent event with transition id/new fingerprint/expected generation but no descriptor digest/final head; derive H1/event id; encode the A-signed transition statement binding H1; require activation-time head to be an authenticated same-incarnation/epoch descendant of H1; in one serialized coordinator transaction recheck zero outstanding A descriptors, append E with the transition digest, and make B current. The first checkpoint covering E is B-signed and references the last registered A checkpoint; no post-E A checkpoint exists. Golden/race vectors cover ordinary intervening events, create-before-register vs register-before-create, competing prepares, descendant/forked heads, exact E boundary, and first-B-checkpoint verification.

**Output-publication registry/event codec:** freeze one domain-tagged `output_publication.v1` record/state machine for backup and encrypted audit-export containers: no record before final authorization; `publishing {opaque_output_id, owner, header/content/target_identity digests, signer key+lineage generation, created sequence}`; `published {file identity/digest, parent-fsync outcome sequence}`; or terminal owner-scoped `abandoned` with reason/confirmation. Unknown state/domain, backward transition, byte/target substitution, publish-after-abandon, and duplicate active ids fail closed. `publishing` and unsigned `published` records participate in U6.8's outstanding-descriptor inventory. G2 vectors and U11 faults cover the two U6.2 transactions around the filesystem rename without claiming cross-domain atomicity.

**Recovery evidence/fork fields:** freeze normal activation and fork-genesis encodings for archive cutoff tuple; `claimed_decommissioned`; source-observation status `barrier_confirmed|last_known|unavailable`; optional observed source tuple/time/provenance; unarchived range/tail digest and complete|partial|unavailable status; RPO status `known_range|unknown` plus acknowledgment digest; supplied checkpoint-set digest; supplied trust-lineage/effective-transition evidence digest; fork-imported public-lineage digest/generation/current signer; target/recipient/assertion bindings; and evidence-completeness enum. Normal activation forbids a post-archive trust import. Fork genesis labels it explicitly and the first new-epoch checkpoint binds that digest. Vectors cover empty/nonempty/unknown tails, later-checkpoint-only, later-lineage-only, both withheld, divergent-incarnation set mixing, wrong effective position, and no cyclic/self-referential fields.

**Canonical publication identity:** `output_publication.v1` stores opaque output id plus domain-separated `artifact_digest = hash(domain || signing_header || encrypted_payload_digest)`, target identity digest, and inner-manifest digest where applicable. Backup calls artifact_digest `<manifest-digest>` and export calls it `<bundle-digest>`; those are aliases of the same frozen field, not different hashes. The field survives missing bytes and is the only abandonment/descriptor key.
notes: Conflict/review: Gate G2 requests positive/negative execution on x86_64 and native/emulated aarch64, while the user explicitly reports ARM64 testing is currently unavailable and automatic CI is disabled for time. Chosen resolution: freeze architecture-independent network-byte-order codecs and deterministic byte fixtures now, run every CI-equivalent check locally on x86_64, retain aarch64 as an honestly externally unverifiable follow-up rather than weaken or fabricate the claim. Rationale: canonical encodings contain no native-endian/platform-dependent operations; pinned vectors and architecture assertion make later ARM evidence byte-comparable. Implementation: authoritative 21-format registry/linter; strict signer-eligibility, maintenance-preflight, output-publication, artifact-identity, recovery-activation, and recovery-family event codecs; emergency credential issuance ID 10; deterministic generator/manifest hashes; normative freeze/sign-off document. Verification: cargo test --locked --test format_registry (6/6), backup_format (6/6), ./scripts/check-crypto-vectors.sh reproduced vectors on x86_64, br dep cycles=0, and ./scripts/verify.sh run_id=01a7746c7767353f73dde578d9fbe10b passed compatibility checks, fixture drift, fmt, strict clippy, all-target/doc tests, Rust 1.85.0 build/tests, harness clippy/tests/MSRV, and canary. Residual: aarch64 execution not performed.

title: M0: Contract freeze (gates G0–G3 closed)
description:
**Milestone M0 — Contract freeze.** Reached when: U0 is complete (client contract captured, Gate G0 closed); U2's redb storage spike has passed with quantitative thresholds recorded (Gate G1 — or the SQLite fallback was triggered and recorded); the authenticated clear-record, crypto-header, and audit-event formats are reviewed and frozen (Gate G2); and the logical-backup format + restore/audit-epoch rules are frozen (Gate G3).

**Why it matters:** these four gates affect persistent formats and security boundaries, not ordinary implementation detail. Candidate fixtures/designs may be built and the data-plane router may proceed after G0 plus its exact component prerequisites. M0 is the point at which reviewed format fixtures become binding release contracts; M1 completion and every public compatibility/release claim remain blocked until M0. U1 configuration/CLI/control-socket/transport work remains parallel.

**Checklist:** G0 closed · G1 spike passed + thresholds recorded (or fallback recorded) · G2 formats frozen with committed vectors · G3 backup format frozen.
**Plan-review wording/parallelism:** candidate fixtures MUST be created/reviewed to close G2/G3 but are not binding release promises until M0 closes. Router implementation may proceed after G0 and its exact component prerequisites; M1 completion and every public compatibility/release claim remain blocked on M0. This matches the graph without weakening any gate.

title: M1: Security-kernel vertical slice — real fnox read E2E
description:
**Milestone M1 — Security-kernel vertical slice.** `init` → `serve` over TLS → the pinned fnox binary resolves a secret end-to-end — through audience-bound credentials (R24/R31), canonical authorization (R8/KTD9), authenticated state (R39), atomic audit (R26), and bounded storage admission (KTD8). A real fnox read at the close of U5.

**A development milestone, not a release.** It proves the security kernel composes: raw-target guard, resource parse, token verify, grant reload inside the transaction, decrypt, audit-before-transmit, TLS. Everything after this layers rotation, recovery, and hardening onto a proven core.

**Evidence:** tests/vertical_slice.rs green — server up over TLS, fnox configured by documentation only, secret round-trips, audit chain carries the read with served version.
**Plan-review evidence:** record pinned fnox version+checksum, immutable server binary digest, harness run directory, stage timings, served version/audit sequence and canary-clean result. Setup is entirely through documented local/public commands—no database fixture insertion—and failure yields a redacted exact reproducer.

**Artifact-safety gate:** M1 evidence is not “canary-clean” by assertion: U5.7 and this milestone consume U11.6, capture raw client/server output privately, and render/retain only after the same-job scan decision. Scanner hit/error/limit fails M1 with sanitized metadata only.
notes: Milestone review conflict inherited from U5.7 (2026-07-17): real pinned fnox 1.30.0 plus OpenBao 2.6.0 succeeds over live rustls and proves raw-target, token, grant, preflight, KV write/read, response, and versioned audit composition, but shipped init/control/serve persistence adapters remain explicitly integration_pending. Therefore this development milestone records a component-composed security-kernel slice, not a claim that documented command-only startup or persistent authenticated audit-chain assembly is finished. Those exact gaps remain visible for final assembly/release review; no public/release claim attaches. Verification: FNOX_E2E_RUN=1 FNOX_E2E_BIN_DIR=/tmp/olss-compat-bin cargo test --locked --test vertical_slice passed; full local verify all run ff3840df6c50d76ee1956ef5cb5fa138 passed with real pinned clients in default and Rust 1.85 all-target tests and canary gate. ARM64 is unavailable and not claimed.

title: M2: Recovery-ready alpha
description:
**Milestone M2 — Recovery-ready alpha.** Reached on top of M1 when ALL pass: fresh-install checkpoint public-trust enrollment plus one old-key-authorized rollover with historical checkpoint/manifest/receipt verification; atomic offline recipient rewrap; `credential epoch rotate` with R17 emergency-control recovery; logical backup with an immutable canonical manifest plus detached signature; both active-recipient and recovery-recipient offline `backup verify --full`; fresh-host restore with credential-epoch bump; rollback restore that opens an explicit audit fork; core transaction/recovery kill points, disk-full and short-write; and R27 recovery.reserve behavior.

**Honest milestone boundary:** M2 consumes the focused U8.4a recovery-key slice, U11.4a core/recovery fault gate, and U11.9r recovery-alpha assembled-binary profile. It intentionally does NOT wait for record-key/metadata/audit-payload rotations, consumer rotation closeout, the extended U11.4 suite, or U11.9 source-full/artifact-smoke; every one remains required by M3. This preserves all coverage while making “recovery-ready alpha” earlier than “rotation-ready release.”

**Claims:** no public artifact may call the project recovery-ready or suitable for real non-production credentials before this gate passes on one immutable release-profile build. Production use remains outside v0.1 pending independent threat-model/crypto/authz/recovery review.

**Assembled-binary evidence:** U11.9r drives init/serve/TLS → signed backup → both rehearsal modes → restore/old-credential rejection/new emergency credential → offline recipient rewrap/restart → epoch rotate → post-archive newer checkpoint + explicit rollback fork. The run manifest records binary digest/arch/stages and canary-clean detailed logs. Source-only kill points are CI evidence from U11.4a; published-byte artifact-smoke is a separate M3/release gate.

**Checklist:** M1 green · signing key generated through approved sink, trust enrolled exactly once, checkpoint signed, lineage rolled once within bounds, historical artifacts still verify · recipient rewrap commit/lock/crash matrix · epoch command invalidates all credential kinds without lockout · signed backup and both rehearsals · AE7 fresh-host restore · real post-archive rollback fork · reserve/control operations at capacity · core fault gate green · recovery-alpha profile green with unconditional artifact safety.

**M2 additions from plan-space audit:** recovery-ready now explicitly includes owner-scoped backup list/show/resume after a lost/ambiguous publication result and real-command recovery.reserve status→release→recreate across online and post-shutdown offline modes. The recovery-alpha profile must prove both without diagnostics privilege, direct DB inspection, hidden hooks, or a blind second create. Unit/fault evidence covers every boundary; assembled evidence proves the release-profile UX.
close_reason: M2 Recovery-ready alpha: all deps closed (U11.4a fault gate, U11.9r recovery-alpha E2E, U8/U10 recovery slices, M1). Local verify: core_recovery_gate, e2e_recovery_alpha, restore/fork/backup, scripts/e2e.sh recovery-alpha PASS.

title: M3: Rotation-ready v0.1 release
description:
**Milestone M3 — Rotation-ready v0.1.** The release milestone. Reached when: declared-consumer reconciliation (R40), begin/cutover/closeout rotation with the guarded completion (R33), the full declared KV surface (R1), the current compatibility matrix (KTD14), immutable staged-candidate artifact-smoke plus post-promotion digest/signature redownload verification, documentation, and release attestations (R38) all pass.

**Definition of Done (global):**
- Every requirement R1–R41 advanced by at least one unit and its cited tests.
- AE1–AE13 pass in CI against the pinned client matrix — the fnox binary included, not only the Vault CLI.
- The full Verification Contract passes: cargo test --locked --all-targets · compat harness (pinned fnox/vault/bao) · clippy -D warnings · fmt --check · cargo deny check · audit-verify vs hand-tampered log · crash/fault suite · property/model suite · scheduled fuzz (incl. archive/export-bundle targets, U11.5b) · secret-output canary scan · differential vs pinned OpenBao (divergences allowlisted with reasons) · performance baseline · clear-state integrity suite · raw-target parser corpus · storage backpressure suite · recovery-epoch suite · consumer-reconciliation matrix · aarch64 cross-arch job · compatibility-doc regeneration drift gate · release integrity · release smoke (the e2e gauntlet including doctor; record, metadata, and audit-payload key rotations with pending anchors; signing-trust rollover; recovery; signed audit export; prior-fixture forward migration; compaction preservation/size reduction; and exact staged/published digest verification on both architectures/filesystems).
- default operation and every production-supported shipped workflow never persist the age identity, first/emergency management credentials, or workload secret_ids to plaintext regular/block storage, shell history, environment, logs, artifacts, or argv; systemd delivery is runtime/tmpfs with encrypted-at-rest persistent provisioning. The explicit unsafe development env compatibility flag is off by default, conspicuously warned, and excluded from docs/release acceptance; third-party env-only use sits outside the custody guarantee. External operator handling beyond approved sinks remains outside the enforceable claim—asserted by tests and diff review.
- Fail-closed postures hold: R18 startup refusals (clock cases included), R20 plaintext refusal, R26 audit atomicity, R39 MAC-failure readiness, R32 restore epoch, R17 disclosure-before-commit, R33 closeout guard.
- No handler commits state or audit outside the transaction coordinator (module visibility + crash suite prove it).
- Fresh-nonce discipline proven under deterministic-RNG suites; no deterministic nonce construction anywhere.
