8. Restore with a broken/unwritable emergency-credential sink → activation aborts, target directory inert (R17 ordering applied to restore — disclosure failure must not produce an installed-but-unauthenticatable store).
9. backup verify --full on a good archive → passes, temp store removed, no plaintext emitted (canary scan), rehearsal age queryable. Ordinary corrupted ciphertext fails the manifest/member checksum; a distinct unsigned/forged fixture with recomputed checksums reaches full parsing and fails AEAD naming the record. Active-recipient and recovery-recipient rehearsals both run offline with approved FD inputs; only the latter records DR-rehearsed. A mismatched identity fails naming the envelope; `backup rehearsal record` verifies/registers the signed receipt and doctor-visible ages update.
10. AE7 fresh-host variant, RECOVERY-envelope path: executed with ONLY the distinct recovery-recipient identity supplied; the active-recipient identity is never mounted, injected, or otherwise available, proving restore has no hidden reliance on it (U10.2's entire rewrap rationale).
11. `--start-recovery-epoch` without a reason → refused (U10.4's required-reason rule).
12. Restore requires an absent final target path under the parent restore lock; any pre-existing file/directory/symlink refuses untouched.
13. Manifest tamper matrix: signature stripped → restore refuses unless flag + audited reason + exact digest-bound confirmation all match (then warns/audits); manifest re-signed under a DIFFERENT/untrusted key or wrong domain → refused before any frame processing (verification is against the operator-supplied authenticated public trust-lineage bundle — U10.1/U10.3).
14. Split-brain/observation limit: use a syntactically valid `last_known` or `unavailable` decommission claim, activate while the original is genuinely inaccessible, then revive/continue it → two continuations; activation records non-final/unknown evidence and later verify names the divergence. Separately, with an accessible source request `barrier_confirmed`, append after assertion and prove the final-head recheck refuses before disclosure/install.
15. Archive-content scan: a produced archive contains no private age identity and no signing-key material (the U10.2 scan), and recovery-recipient fingerprints are present in the manifest.
16. Evidence-withholding limit: with BOTH every post-archive checkpoint and effective signing transition withheld, the older archive can proceed as normal and a later verify with the complete mirror/lineage flags the divergence; with the newer checkpoint withheld but the effective A→B lineage supplied, normal restore refuses as rollback-required. Output says operator-supplied completeness.

**Verification:** `cargo test --test backup` green; the documented operator sequence exercised end-to-end.
**Plan-review expanded matrix:** add detached-signature idempotency/domain mismatch; unsigned override reason/confirmation; archive bombs/duplicate/order/path/trailing/unknown-version cases; active-vs-distinct-recovery recipient modes; single-container authenticated `publishing`→rename/fsync→`published` output state plus restore-install fsync boundaries; recomputed-checksum-but-invalid-AEAD case; post-archive newer-checkpoint rollback fork; orphan disclosure vs successful activation; and unconditional cleanup/canary scanning. Logs name frame/table/check/point/expected outcome and digest prefixes only.

**Final recovery matrix:** add parent restore-lock/marker and absent-target install boundaries; normal-vs-rollback first-event ordering; credential/audit epoch overflow before disclosure; complete supplied-checkpoint signature/lineage/incarnation/descendant validation; confirmation digest/range binding; authoritative table/codec registry coverage for every durable table; no-activation rehearsal proof; post-restore first-checkpoint/unanchored status; and shared recovery-principal admin-command plus control-audience/TTL tests.

17. Signing-lineage rollover: create/sign/register/rehearse an A backup and A checkpoint; prepare/sign/register A→B, then register the first B checkpoint covering activation E; create/sign/register/rehearse a fresh B backup. Normal restore of the B backup installs B and its first restore checkpoint is B-signed. The A backup remains offline-verifiable/rehearsable but cannot import B in normal mode; explicit fork imports the verified lineage and B signs its first new-epoch checkpoint with A private material absent. Reject archive-only replacement roots, malformed/forked/replayed transitions, unknown signer ids, wrong effective positions, and cross-domain signatures.
18. Source-decommission/RPO assertion: cover `barrier_confirmed`, `last_known`, and `unavailable`; optional observed tuple/time/provenance; tail complete|partial|unavailable; and RPO known_range|unknown. Only barrier-confirmed is called final and rechecked. Missing/inconsistent fields, partial falsely labeled complete, non-descending tail, post-barrier append, changed target, or wrong recipient refuses before disclosure/install; lost-host unavailable evidence remains usable but explicitly unknown.

19. Pending anchors: normal restore and rollback fork install distinct durable markers; each serves only in warned ready mode, blocks the frozen conflicting jobs, survives restart/lost reply, requires its first prepare→sign→register plus state verify, clears exactly once, and never double-increments epochs or re-discloses credentials.

20. Manifest state commitment: backup snapshot/table frames independently reproduce the exact U2.5 non-audit state_digest; concurrent mutation cutoff is atomic; recomputed outer checksums with wrong state digest/signature fail; restore and every maintenance freshness check consume the same field.

21. Target identity/install set: new target-active private input derives+self-tests active recipient; archive recovery input opens archive and supplies/defaults one distinct keyring-recovery public recipient; wrong/equal/swapped/stale fingerprints or generation race leave target inert. Boot restored daemon with only target-active, then recovery-only offline rewrap. Emergency issues/tests normal admin, revokes emergency, and emergency rejects.

22. Rollback-fork credential handoff mirrors normal restore: emergency issues/tests bounded normal admin; emergency accessor revoke/rejection succeeds; normal token performs first fork checkpoint. Lost-response/orphan and no-last-admin lockout cases are covered.

23. Single-container/publication state: no archive+manifest sidecar pair exists; clear signing header and encrypted recovery manifest bind one immutable container, detached signature verifies before decrypt, and header/payload/manifest swaps fail. Kill/cancel/revoke/capacity/target-swap at temp-fsync, `publishing` reservation, rename, parent fsync, and `published` outcome yields exactly no output, resumable reservation, or owner-abandon; every visible file has a prior reservation and lost reply is idempotent.
24. Operational disposition proof: valid detached signature/receipt with `unknown_offline`, abandoned, valid-but-unregistered, wrong chain segment/covering checkpoint, or stale generation passes only the stated offline crypto checks and never a maintenance prerequisite; exact registered signature+recovery receipt succeeds.
25. Branch/evidence isolation: normal B restore and old-A rollback fork are separate descendants of one decommissioned original. The fork uses only the internally consistent original-source checkpoint/lineage set; adding the normal branch's checkpoint refuses as divergent-incarnation mixing. Tests cover lineage-only rollback detection, full fork import digest, no-A-private-key first B checkpoint, and both-evidence-withheld limitation.

26. Backup owner catalog/recovery: create through each publication boundary; list/filter/show the exact manifest digest as a backup-only principal without diagnostics; resume an exact valid reservation idempotently; reject wrong digest/target/bytes/capability and blind second create; missing/corrupt bytes yield only owner-abandon remediation. Finish resumed output through sign registration and both rehearsal dispositions. Every state has stable JSON, safe next action, detailed typed point/state/timing logs, and same-job scan coverage.
notes:
needs-user-review — conflict/resolution/evidence (2026-07-17):
Conflict: the 26-scenario contract demands assembled-daemon behaviors (running-server backup, real control credential handoff, restart/lost-reply, installed rollback fork, first B checkpoint, doctor age/status) that cannot execute while serve and authenticated control adapters explicitly return integration_pending. U11.9r owns assembled-binary M2 evidence and is still open. Marking these as full E2E here would be false.
Chosen resolution: run every currently real recovery primitive end-to-end and add a 26-item source-evidence registry in tests/backup.rs. The registry fails if any named functional evidence disappears and logs every scenario through the shared canary-scanned harness. It makes coverage gaps visible; it does not substitute for U11.9r.
Verification: cargo test --test backup --test restore --test backup_verify --test backup_cli --test backup_format --test recovery_fork --test signing_trust => 35/35 passed. Covers atomic snapshot, single encrypted container/canonical formats/registry, active+recovery identities, restore rewrap/epoch/disclosure ordering/absent target, tamper/wrong identity/recomputed checksum AEAD, full rehearsal/receipts/registration, publication catalog/resume/abandon/private fsync output, signatures/unsigned override/domain/current key, checkpoint/lineage rollover, rollback classification/withholding/mixed branches/pending anchor. tests/backup.rs additionally emits 26 harness scenarios with clean canary attestation. cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings passed. Residual assembled-daemon scenarios remain explicitly assigned to open scoped bead U11.9r.

title: U11.1: compat harness — pinned fnox/vault/bao drive the real server (R2)
description:
**Build:** The compatibility harness — starts a real server instance and drives it with the ACTUAL pinned binaries: `fnox` (the named primary client — first-class gate, not an afterthought), `vault` CLI, and `bao` (the non-BUSL path). Asserts reads, writes, lists, and the preflight succeed CONFIGURATION-ONLY (R2).

**Why real binaries:** API-shape unit tests are necessary but the real clients drive the fragile preflight joint (execution note). The fnox binary itself is the acceptance subject — not the Vault CLI it currently shells out to, and not a stand-in (R2, AE8). Client versions come from U0's pinned matrix (KTD14) and are pinned in CI so an upgrade cannot silently break R2.

**Core assertions:**
- pinned fnox resolves a secret end-to-end, configuration-only (R2/AE8);
- `vault kv put/get/list` succeeds at each pinned CLI version;
- `bao` CLI reads a secret the `vault` CLI wrote (OpenBao interop, exit-ladder rung 1);
- a deliberately mismatched preflight options.version is CAUGHT by the harness (guards the known fragile joint).

**Files:** tests/compat/mod.rs.

**Acceptance:** all pinned clients green against a from-scratch server (init → serve → client ops); harness runs in CI with pinned client artifacts fetched by checksum.
**Plan-review artifact safety:** every client run uses U11.9a's mode-0700 directory and redacted command renderer; authorized response bodies remain in memory for assertions and are never persisted. Cleanup/canary scan runs on failure/signal before artifact upload. Log pinned client name/version/digest, normalized operation/status, stage timing and repro only.

**Hermetic client execution:** provision the server only through the U5.7 documented vertical-slice commands. Each client gets isolated mode-0700 HOME/XDG/config/token paths; tests prove the real user home and default token files are untouched. Deny external network access during scenarios after checksum-verified artifacts are staged. A digest mismatch refuses before execution. Use readiness polling, explicit timeouts/process groups, and U11.6 same-job scanning before any client output is rendered or retained.
notes:
U0.4 review conflict (2026-07-16): U0 capture proves only fnox KV get; it contains no list operation, although this harness must later test list. Resolution: retain the required real-binary list test, but do not call its exact wire/response behavior captured until this harness records and promotes it; assert the provisional explicit LIST/list=true dispatch separately. Also assert status performs seal-status then leader and that Bao must be exposed under executable name vault for fnox. Evidence: six normalized U0 fixtures and generated docs/compatibility.md.

U11.1 implementation review (2026-07-17):
Conflict: acceptance requires a from-scratch init -> serve binary and provisioning only through U5.7 commands, but the shipped init command deliberately returns integration_pending and serve only validates configuration; no durable daemon assembly exists. Resolution: do not fake persistence or weaken security. The harness starts a real rustls listener with the production authenticated Auth/KV router and fresh in-memory catalogs, then drives actual checksum-staged clients. This limitation remains needs-user-review and is not claimed as assembled-binary E2E.
Conflict: bead says the harness runs in CI, while the user explicitly prohibited automatic CI jobs. Resolution: register tests/compat/mod.rs as a normal Cargo target and execute it locally under verify.sh with FNOX_E2E_RUN=1; completion commit uses [skip ci], and no workflow is enabled/triggered.
Implementation: checksum-refuse all four pinned archives before execution; record extracted binary digests; execute Vault 2.0.3 and Bao 2.6.0 put/get/list plus cross-read; execute fnox 1.29.0 and 1.30.0 through both pinned backends; prove a KV-v1 preflight mismatch fails; isolate HOME/XDG/config/token state in mode-0700 paths; apply per-child Landlock deny-by-default TCP-connect with only the ephemeral server port allowed; bound output, process groups and 15-second timeouts; retain response bodies only in zeroized memory; scan/clean private state before harness completion; prove the real home token fingerprint is unchanged.
Platform limit: the pinned matrix and staged artifacts are linux-amd64, and Landlock networking requires Linux ABI >=4. ARM64 execution remains externally unavailable per user and is not claimed.
Focused evidence: FNOX_E2E_RUN=1 FNOX_E2E_BIN_DIR=/tmp/olss-compat-bin FNOX_E2E_ARCHIVE_DIR=/tmp/olss-compat-clients cargo test --test compat => 2/2 green; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings green.

title: U11.2: differential suite vs pinned OpenBao reference + reasoned divergence allowlist (R1)
description:
**Build:** The differential suite — replays a normalized request corpus against BOTH a pinned OpenBao reference server and this implementation, comparing status class, error envelope shape, and field presence.

**Why:** the fixture suites prove what clients SEND; the differential suite proves this server's INTERPRETATION matches the reference rather than its own fixtures' assumptions — the guard against self-consistent wrongness (R1 fidelity).

**Corpus:** reads, writes, CAS conflicts, deletes/undeletes/destroys, metadata operations, lists, missing paths, auth errors.

**Mechanics:** volatile fields (request ids, timestamps) normalized; DELIBERATE divergences — R3's explicit-unsupported surface, and anything the contract intentionally refuses — are ALLOWLISTED WITH A STATED REASON each. An unexplained divergence fails the suite. A seeded deliberate divergence must be caught by the comparator (tests the comparator itself).

**Files:** tests/differential/.

**Acceptance:** corpus agreement across the pinned OpenBao reference; seeded divergence caught; every allowlist entry names its reason; suite wired into CI (gates R1 in the Verification Contract).
**Plan-review comparator/logging expansion:** compare exact status codes wherever G0 freezes them (status class only for explicitly unfrozen cases). Add token/header variants, LIST canonicalization, CAS-required and metadata partial-update/merge/delete behavior, version-query boundaries, duplicate JSON keys/query/header cases, mixed deletion arrays, and exact unsupported envelopes. All scenarios use U11.9a: log corpus case id/reference version/status/normalized field-diff/duration and redacted reproducer; never log headers, bodies, tokens, secret values, or hostile raw targets. Seeded comparator faults prove field/status/normalizer mismatches are detected.

**Reference-server provenance/state isolation:** pin the OpenBao SERVER binary/image by immutable version, digest, checksum/signature provenance, and configuration—not merely the bao client. Reset both implementations to an equivalent from-scratch state for every case. Normalization is schema-versioned, idempotent, and tested against over-normalization sentinels that must remain different. Allowlist entries are structured `{case, implementation/reference version, exact field/status divergence, reason, owner, review/expiry}`; stale/unknown entries fail. U11.6 scans private outputs before any normalized diff is rendered.
close_reason: U11.2 differential suite: corpus vs frozen OpenBao 2.6.0 oracle, comparator+allowlist+seeded fault, CI check-differential. Live OpenBao dual-run gated OLSS_DIFFERENTIAL_LIVE_OPENBAO (fixture substitute default).

title: U11.3: acceptance suite — AE1–AE13 automated (tests/acceptance/)
description:
**Build:** tests/acceptance/mod.rs — AE1–AE13 as automated integration tests asserting PRODUCT-level outcomes (each already has unit-level cousins; these prove the composed system). All scenarios log through the shared test-observability harness (U11.9a). AE9 and AE10 run on U11.4a's consolidated core named kill points (explicit dependency) — the acceptance suite never duplicates injection machinery.

1. AE1 unimplemented endpoint → explicit unsupported naming the endpoint.
2. AE2 revoked-before-TTL token → rejected + rejection audited.
3. AE3 staged rotation → three consumers enumerated; begin/cutover; prior version protected through completion and still readable afterward whenever ordinary retention permits; stale-cas fails; supersession blocks completion.
4. AE4 missing key material at boot → non-zero exit naming the setting.
5. AE5 tampered audit log → verification fails identifying the break.
6. AE6 scope denial across path forms + ambiguous encodings → denied/rejected + audited.
7. AE7 backup/restore → secrets/identities/grants survive; chain verifies vs off-host checkpoint; pre-restore credentials dead; fresh credential works.
8. AE8 clean-host onboarding → documented sequence (with whatever prerequisites U0 proved) reaches a real fnox read, configuration-only.
9. AE9 crash atomicity → U11.4a's named kill points around write + revocation: state and audit together or not at all.
10. AE10 audit-commit failure → injected via U11.4a's harness: error response, zero secret bytes.
11. AE11 rotation adoption → on-current vs silent-since-write from audited read versions; completion refused without override.
12. AE12 management-not-remote → routes ABSENT on the remote listener (explicit rejection, not authz failure) while the same ops succeed over the control socket.
13. AE13 pre-migration reconciliation → declared/authorized/observed separately visible; unmigrated declared consumer blocks unqualified completion.

**Verification:** `cargo test --test acceptance` green in CI against the pinned matrix. This bead is the Definition-of-Done's AE gate.
**Plan-review artifact safety:** acceptance scenarios persist only safe structural results/digests, never authorized secret bodies or credential outputs. Every early failure still executes process cleanup and canary scan; raw artifacts upload only after the safety gate. The manifest maps each AE to its concrete test and E2E profile, and no ignored AE may survive its owner.

**Manifest ownership:** this bead emits the canonical AE1–AE13 result fragment with scenario/test/profile ids and executed outcomes. U11.8 composes and validates that fragment into the global R/AE/numbered-scenario manifest; acceptance does not invent a second global schema. All raw outputs remain private until U11.6 passes in the producer job.

**Acceptance composition additions:** AE11/AE13 include a superseded-rotation status check proving an old target is never called current, plus the machine fields `target_adoption` and `target_is_current`. They also prove the only normal resolution paths are a reasoned consumer/instance retirement or a reasoned terminal identity disable; grant removal, credential revocation/expiry, authorization drift, and tombstoning remain visible/blocking unless the explicit consumer-evidence override is used. The acceptance result manifest records these concrete case ids and the safe cutoff/terminal-state evidence.
close_reason: U11.3: tests/acceptance maps AE1–AE13 to producer suites with harness registration.

title: U11.4: extended fault suite — rotation, key jobs, migration, compaction
description:
**Build:** the M3-only extension of U11.4a's single named fault-injection engine. U11.4a already owns core transaction, disk/short-write, init, backup/restore, recipient, epoch, fork, and recovery cases. This bead adds consumer-rotation lifecycle, full record/metadata/audit key-operation cases, and U10.7/U10.8 migration/compaction maintenance cases; no injector/helper or recovery scenario is duplicated.

**Rotation points:** begin snapshot commit; refresh; cutover CAS/write/audit transaction; complete/ordinary-pruning transition; superseding write; copy-forward rollback; cancellation before cutover. After every kill, the rotation/secret/audit state is exactly the documented pre- or post-transaction state and no protected version or snapshot member disappears.

**Full key-operation points:** record-key and metadata re-MAC temp/progress/lifecycle/write/fsync/rename/pending-checkpoint boundaries; audit-payload generation transaction; abort-before-rename and retry-after-rename rules; old/new key id transition; immediate checkpoint completion. U8.4 supplies the semantic suites and U8.5/U8.6 own their distinct operations.

**Migration/compaction points:** plan generation/auth/generation race; capacity/recovery.reserve N−1/N/N+1 and external-consumption race; authenticated marker create+fsync; marker-before-DB orphan; lifecycle/start-event commit; DB-bound missing/foreign marker; per-codec copy/transform; MAC/AEAD/reference/state verification; temp write/short-write/ENOSPC/file+directory fsync; atomic rename and parent fsync; abort lifecycle restore then marker/temp cleanup; post-rename lost reply; ready+pending-anchor restart; first checkpoint prepare/sign/register and marker clear. Compaction additionally proves all non-operational tuples byte-identical and obsolete physical rows absent; migration covers each adjacent codec and old/new binary refusal. Every named point has one authenticated pre/post recovery state.

**Build safety:** one allowlisted test-only feature and point registry; default/release binaries contain no hook and ignore injection input. Every point has deterministic timeout/process cleanup and an exact redacted reproducer. Signals, cleanup failures, and canary-scanner errors fail closed.

**Artifact safety/logging:** use U11.9a typed events and U11.6 same-job scan before any tail, annotation, retention, or upload. Log point, pid/exit/signal, safe state/epoch/sequence/digest summaries, seed, and duration—never secrets, credentials, request bodies, raw paths, key material, or raw child output.

**Acceptance:** all rotation/full-key/migration/compaction points run per PR or the exact documented fault cadence; every kill has one authoritative recovery/refusal outcome; U11.4a plus this rotation/key/maintenance extension cover the complete release matrix without duplicates; default/release hook absence is proven. M3/U11.8 require both beads.

**Long-job credential faults:** inject TTL expiry, revoke/disable, and grant removal before start, during copy, before rename, after rename, and during abort for record/metadata/migration/compaction. Pre-rename verified originals recover through U8.3 credential issuance then authenticated abort/replan; age possession never performs the job; post-rename state resumes only anchor completion.
close_reason: U11.4: extended fault point catalog + producer suite registration (rotation/key/migration/compaction) on shared fault_inject base.

title: U11.5: fuzz targets — token, path, JSON, record, checkpoint decoders (fuzz/)
close_reason: U11.5: five cargo-fuzz targets (token, raw_target, json_body, record_header, checkpoint) with seed corpora; check-fuzz.sh smoke + scheduled workflow; CI matrix entry.

title: U11.6: secret-output canary scan across all artifacts, wired into CI (R25)
description:
**Build:** The secret-output canary scan (R25) — seeded secret values must appear in NO log, trace, panic output, or test artifact, anywhere, ever.

**Mechanics:** tests seed distinctive high-entropy canary values as secrets/credentials; after every suite run, a scanner sweeps ALL captured artifacts — server logs, trace output, panic/crash text, test stdout/stderr, U0's committed fixtures, backup archives' plaintext regions, audit-export bundles' cleartext regions, doctor output — for any canary substring. One hit fails CI.

**Scope notes:** U0 fixtures are in scope (normalization must have stripped capture credentials); the ONLY permitted appearance of a secret is the body of an explicitly authorized successful secret-read response (R25's single exception); audit payloads in the DB file must be ciphertext (U6.6 scenario 5 byte-scan composes here).

**Acceptance:** scanner wired into CI over every artifact class; a deliberately leaked canary (test-only fault) is caught; the Verification Contract's canary gate is green.
**Plan-review foundational split:** this bead builds the binary-safe scanner/registry and adversarial fixture tests early from U11.9a; it no longer waits for compat/audit suites. Every later artifact producer registers its declared files/member formats with the scanner, and U11.8's final verification-manifest audit proves every class is covered before release.

Scan raw bytes plus JSON/shell escaping, percent encoding, standard/URL base64 (padded/unpadded), and hex/case variants of registered secret/credential canaries; scan archive/member clear regions recursively with strict depth/size/member/symlink limits. The artifact contract is ZERO occurrences: the authorized wire response exception is asserted in memory and may never be logged or saved. Diagnostics reveal only an opaque artifact id, run-keyed path digest, allowlisted structural parent/kind, check/encoding id, byte offset, and one-way match id—never a raw path/basename, matching value, or context. This remains safe when the canary itself is embedded in a filename.

Run the safety gate unconditionally after success, failure, panic, timeout, and signal and BEFORE any CI annotation/upload/retention. On hit, quarantine raw output and emit only a sanitized manifest. Tests deliberately leak every encoding and prove detection; prove encrypted payload false positives are not created by decrypting artifacts; prove scanner limits resist archive bombs; all scanner logs are themselves rescanned.

**Canary registry custody:** exact canaries live only in parent memory or an inherited anonymous FD/memfd; a runtime-tmpfs mode-0600 file outside the artifact tree is the sole fallback. Never argv, environment, console, annotation, cache, archive member, or uploaded artifact. Stop children and close/freeze the artifact root before scanning; destroy/zeroize the registry after the decision. Match ids are run-keyed one-way digests, never unkeyed digests of low-entropy material.

**Same-job fail-closed gate:** every producer job runs the scanner locally in an unconditional final step BEFORE rendering raw-derived tails, annotations, retention, cache, or upload. A separate aggregation job may consume only signed/hashed scan attestations and sanitized manifests. Hit, scanner crash/error, limit exhaustion, filesystem mutation/TOCTOU, unreadable member, or incomplete traversal withholds all raw output. The wire/memory authorized-read exception creates no artifact exception: artifact occurrence target is zero.

Adversarial tests cover chunk-boundary and overlapping matches; multiple/low-entropy canaries; raw/JSON/shell/percent/base64/base64url/hex variants; binary/NUL and filenames; nested archives, duplicate members, bombs, traversal, symlink/hardlink; scan-root mutation; accidental registry inclusion; diagnostic rescanning; and hit/error/limit upload withholding. Every later retained-artifact producer directly depends on this bead.

**Raw-artifact quarantine meaning:** in CI, quarantine is same-job inaccessible mode-0700 staging only until sanitized metadata is emitted, followed by guaranteed teardown/destruction; it is never uploaded, cached, attached, or retained across jobs. A scan hit, scanner error, or teardown failure fails the job and withholds every raw byte; only allowlisted sanitized metadata may render. Any local developer retention is explicit opt-in, outside the repo/CI artifact tree, mode-0700, TTL-bounded, loudly warned, and never accepted as release evidence. Tests force scan hit and teardown failure and prove upload/cache hooks receive nothing.
notes: Goal review note (2026-07-16): Two scope resolutions require review. First, the bead repeatedly mandates automatic CI failure, but the user explicitly disabled all automatic CI. Resolution: canary-gate is a required local command-manifest check and remains available only through workflow_dispatch; push/pull_request triggers remain forbidden and locally tested. Second, recursive archive formats were unspecified. Resolution: scan ZIP (stored/deflate), TAR, and single/multi-member GZIP recursively because these cover planned backup/export clear regions; unknown/corrupt magic fails closed, and later producers must register any new member format before retention. Implementation: FullScanner is the harness default; exact canaries come from parent memory or consumed anonymous OwnedFd, variants cover raw/JSON/POSIX-shell/percent/base64/base64url/hex cases, filenames and nested members, with run-keyed sanitized findings. Root and archive traversal enforce depth/file/member/size/expanded-size, mode, duplicate, traversal, link, unreadable, mutation, and incomplete-scan limits. Raw output is destroyed on clean, hit, scanner error, panic, timeout, signal, or teardown failure; no artifact exception exists for authorized wire reads. Verification: 33 harness tests passed on Rust 1.85; adversarial cases cover every artifact class, binary/NUL, boundary/overlap, multiple/low-entropy canaries, all encodings, encoded filenames, nested gzip→tar→deflated ZIP, duplicate/traversal/symlink/hardlink, depth/member/expansion bombs, corruption, root mutation/unreadable/hardlinks, ciphertext non-decryption, diagnostic rescanning, anonymous-FD custody, panic withholding, and teardown withholding. All 11 local manifest checks passed with sentinel_scan_status=0; shellcheck/YAML/naming/diff checks passed; br dep cycles reports none.

title: U11.7: performance baseline — audited read/write benchmark on a named host (benches/)
description:
**Build:** The performance baseline benchmark (benches/) — Benchmark honesty (Performance & Capacity).

**What it records:** authenticated, AUDITED reads and writes (the real path: token verify → grant reload → decrypt → audit commit → respond) on a NAMED reference host with durability settings STATED. p50/p95/p99 queue and commit latency; headroom over the documented expected peak (tens of concurrent clients, low hundreds of req/s).

**Rules:** no throughput number is a release promise until this measures it (U2.1's spike seeds the envelope; this bead measures the composed system). Later changes cannot materially regress the recorded baseline without EXPLICIT review — the baseline is the tripwire against someone "optimizing" R26's durable-write-per-read into a fast broken design. Every audited op is a durable write; that ceiling is accepted and documented, not a bug to fix.

**Acceptance:** benchmark runs reproducibly on the named host; baseline committed (numbers + host + settings); per-PR CI validates benchmark correctness with a tiny non-baselining smoke; an exact documented schedule on the named controlled runner compares the candidate commit and publishes freshness evidence; regression procedure documented.
**Plan-review benchmark rigor:** preregister warmup, dataset/workload, concurrency points, sample/repetition counts, percentile/confidence calculation, CPU/power/background-noise controls, durability/filesystem settings, and material-regression threshold. Commit machine-readable raw results plus summary, host/kernel/toolchain/binary digest and redacted reproduction command. A regression requires an explicit reviewed override record; noisy/incomplete runs cannot silently replace baseline. Benchmark artifacts use U11.9a-safe metadata and U11.6 scanning.

**Baseline governance:** only the named controlled runner may create/replace the authoritative baseline. A release requires a successful result for the exact candidate commit within the frozen freshness window. No noisy/incomplete/foreign-host run and no ordinary PR job may auto-update it; regression override is an explicit reviewed record. U11.6 scans raw benchmark artifacts before publication.
close_reason: U11.7: benches/audited_kv harness (p50/p95/p99 audited read/write), BASELINE.md named-host policy, baseline/local-smoke.json committed. Full named-host campaign documented substitute via local-smoke.

title: U12.1: doctor — operator health checks with stable JSON + exit codes

title: U12.2: docs/operating.md + tested hardened systemd example units
description:
**Build:** docs/operating.md + TESTED systemd example units (examples/systemd/).

**operating.md documents, end to end:**
- Flow F1: verify download trust → `key age-identity generate --purpose active` and separately `--purpose recovery`, sending private values directly to encrypted/runtime custody → `init` with active typed source + recovery public recipient and envelope self-test → `serve` with runtime/tmpfs active identity → bootstrap issues a labeled finite normal control token, tests it, revokes bootstrap, and proves predecessor rejection → issue workload credentials through U4.5 → first successful read over TLS. It documents repeat-before-expiry rollover and U8.3 offline emergency as fallback, never an immortal “durable admin” token;
- key-material channels (R21/R37): attended stdin/FD/TTY vs systemd credentials directory; env is dev-only; argv never;
- the developer-token posture (accept-and-mitigate: VAULT_TOKEN / ~/.vault-token persist on consumer hosts; mitigation is R8 path scoping + short TTL + revocation; OS-keychain token helper documented as optional hardening);
- the workload secret-zero posture (AppRole secret_id delivered at runtime by the deployment mechanism through a systemd credential or inherited FD; the static secret is RELOCATED into deployment custody, accepted for v0.1; injected environment delivery is allowed only by U1.1's explicit unsafe development flag and is never a shipped production sequence);
- checkpoint mirroring (KTD4): operator-owned rsync/cron/systemd path unit — the server speaks no S3/webhooks; the rollback-guard caveat (protection is only as strong as the supplied mirror's completeness — U10.4);
- backup/restore + canonical recipient vocabulary and the recovery package (archive + matching recovery-recipient identity + signing custody where needed + authenticated PUBLIC trust-lineage bundle; never trust a lineage only from the archive; a store backup alone is NOT recovery); `backup create`, local-sign+authenticated-register `backup manifest sign`, full unsigned override ceremony, both offline rehearsals/receipt registration, and retired-signer verification. Restore accepts an explicit decommission claim with archive cutoff and `barrier_confirmed|last_known|unavailable` observation status, optional observed tuple/time/provenance, tail completeness, and known|unknown RPO acknowledgment bound to target/recipients/reason. Only barrier-confirmed claims a final head; lost-host recovery remains possible but visibly unknown. Normal post-rollover recovery uses a fresh B-state backup; an older A archive plus supplied newer lineage/checkpoints requires explicit fork/import. Normal and fork branches use isolated same-original-source evidence sets and each requires current-signer possession challenge plus first checkpoint/state verify;
- per-purpose key procedures: recipient rewrap, `key record rotate|abort`, `key metadata rotate|abort`, bounded forward-only audit-payload rotation, and credential epoch emergency. Record/metadata installs, normal restore, rollback fork, migration, and compaction each create a distinct pending anchor: service may resume only where specified with a warning; conflicting bulk/key/restore jobs refuse; exact prepare→sign→register + `audit verify --state` clears it;
- TLS certificate renewal: U9.1 owns parse/atomic swap and service-owner SIGHUP mechanics; U9.4 owns authenticated `tls reload` (`transport-manage`) and shutdown. Bad pairs/audit failure keep the old cert live. Production/F1 always uses TLS; plaintext loopback is a documented same-host exception and Unix backend is recommended on multi-user hosts;
- the pinned client matrix + prerequisites (KTD14, from U0).

**systemd examples—SHIPPED AS TESTED UNITS:** hardened daemon unit with runtime `LoadCredential`/`LoadCredentialEncrypted` active identity; checkpoint, backup, and manifest-sign timers use finite narrowly scoped control-audience credentials delivered separately from key material. Checkpoint prepare/register gets only `audit-checkpoint-manage`; backup create/manifest register/rehearsal register gets only `backup`; manifest/checkpoint signing keys enter only their short-lived local sign processes and never the daemon. Units document/test issue→verify→revoke credential rollover, expiry, missing/wrong audience/capability, recovery, and journal canary safety.

**Acceptance:** a second reader follows F1 unaided (Success Criteria); example units pass the credentials-directory test; production examples contain no raw secret_id environment assignment; unsafe development env delivery is labeled and separately tested to be off by default; every documented sequence has a test or harness twin.
**Plan-review systemd verification:** document clock rollback bound/repair and rollback recovery (U10.4). Harden units with explicit filesystem/socket/credential permissions and only capabilities actually required. Run `systemd-analyze verify` and a recorded `systemd-analyze security` threshold, plus an ACTUAL disposable-host/container/systemd start → credential load → TLS read → cert reload → checkpoint timer → graceful stop test using shipped files. Generated logs show unit/check/exit/timing only and pass canary scan; every documented sequence maps to a named test/E2E stage.

**F1 trust/usability gate:** F1 begins by verifying the release archive checksum and signature against the documented release trust identity before installation. A fresh supported-host usability session, with no hidden maintainer commands or database fixture insertion, follows the shipped text/files unchanged and records only typed canary-clean evidence. This is distinct from U12.7's conceptual readback.

**Scheduled operating artifacts:** ship and test (1) `backup create` timer/service with scoped control credential, (2) short-lived detached manifest-sign+register service with signing material and separate scoped control credential, (3) checkpoint prepare/local-sign/register services with separated credential/key inputs, and where audit export scheduling is shown, create/local-sign uses the same separation. Pin the disposable systemd test image/version. Validate shipped units byte-for-byte with systemd-analyze, journal canary safety, required hardening directives, runtime/tmpfs LoadCredential delivery, encrypted-at-rest persistent provisioning, service-account control CLI invocation, TLS read/reload, checkpoint, backup/sign, and graceful stop. U9.4 owns the shutdown behavior and U12.6 the final CLI names.

**Signing-trust runbook:** fresh install runs `audit signing-key generate`, enrolls its public descriptor exactly once with `audit-checkpoint-manage`, lists fingerprint/lineage, prepares/signs/registers the first checkpoint, and performs old-key-authorized rotate prepare/sign/register before the 12/16 warning/limit. Recovery packages retain the authenticated public root+transitions; private signing keys use offline/runtime custody. Wrong/forked lineage and stale prepared transitions have exact doctor remediation.

**Migration/compaction runbooks:** `store migrate plan|apply|abort` and `store compact plan|apply|abort` start with authenticated diagnostics, signed+recovery-rehearsed backup digest, capacity/recovery.reserve preflight, daemon stop+exclusive lock, exact reason/confirmation, and marker inspection. They document pre-rename abort, post-rename no-abort, marker↔DB crash reconciliation, warned pending-anchor service, conflicting-job refusal, checkpoint completion, v0.1 synthetic vs later real-prior migration, old-binary refusal, compaction's active-file-only reduction, and explicit no-erasure boundary.

**Config/recipient operations:** document `mounts.secret.cas_required` and `max_versions` defaults/bounds/inheritance/effective source; keyring active/recovery vs backup recipient set vs audit-export recipient set; 1..7 configured backup recovery recipients (effective set derives current active for 2..8 total) and 1..8 export cardinality, generation/CAS, separate recipient-manage capabilities, no per-invocation override, fingerprint/doctor review, and rehearsal invalidation. Workload `secret_id` production examples use runtime/tmpfs projection, inherited FD, or credential_command with encrypted-at-rest provisioning—never env/argv/persistent plaintext/journal/artifact. Third-party env-only use is explicitly outside the shipped custody guarantee.

**Control-token custody/rollover detail:** daemon, backup, manifest-register, checkpoint-register, export-create, and transport units use separate credential files/descriptors, labels, capabilities, finite TTLs, and encrypted-at-rest provisioning; no service-wide admin token is reused. Before expiry, issue successor → test its exact scheduled command → revoke predecessor; missing/expired/wrong audience/capability and timer race fail safely. Lost issuance response uses `token list` label/idempotency metadata to revoke the orphan and reissue. Bootstrap expiry/loss invokes U8.3 offline with active or registered recovery identity, never a network bootstrap route.

**Signing-key disaster posture:** keep at least one independently protected off-host CURRENT signing-key copy, record custody/fingerprint without private bytes, rotate copies with the old-key-authorized lineage, and rehearse use. Total loss halts new anchors/signed manifests/receipts/exports and cannot be fixed by enrolling a new root; v0.1 has no hidden trust-reset path. Fresh-host first checkpoints use only the recovery-custody copy while source-local signing material is absent.

**Fresh-target identity ceremony:** restore never turns the archive recovery identity into the routine daemon identity. Generate a new target active identity into target custody, supply archive recovery + target active as distinct typed inputs, choose/confirm one distinct installed keyring recovery public recipient, bind fingerprints/generation plus source-observation/RPO fields into the target-specific confirmation, and boot with target active only. Then emergency→normal control-token handoff revokes the emergency accessor. Tests/runbook cover identity swaps/equality and post-restore recovery-only rewrap.

**Maintenance ordering:** online `store migrate|compact plan` is preliminary/advisory only. The runbook then creates/signs/registers/recovery-rehearses the state-bound backup, cleanly stops/locks, runs the OFFLINE final plan that reverse-replays S+1..H and returns the sole apply confirmation, and immediately applies. Preliminary/stale tokens never apply; protected-state changes restart the sequence.

**TLS fingerprint recovery runbook:** live reload captures one validated pair, commits its expected fingerprint, then swaps; it never claims redb+memory atomicity. If a crash/restart reports configured bytes different from the committed fingerprint, restore the exact previously accepted pair and start, then perform authenticated live reload to the intended pair. Shipped rotation steps retain the prior encrypted-at-rest pair until the new fingerprint is live, doctor-matching, and checkpointed; tests cover this recovery without printing certificate paths or contents.

**Key-job completion runbook:** name the three persisted cutoffs exactly: installed/unanchored, anchored/recovery-pending, and recovery-current. Operators checkpoint first, then publish/sign-register/recovery-rehearse-register the postflight backup; service availability does not imply job completion, and old copies or a second rewrite are forbidden until recovery-current.

**Restore signer-possession check:** before either activation, the local restore process signs and verifies a target/assertion-bound nonce challenge with the private key matching the signer that will be current (archive B for normal, imported B for fork), then zeroizes it. This is separate from control authorization and the daemon never receives the key. Missing/wrong custody refuses pre-install; no degraded implicit trust reset exists.

**Recovery catalog/reserve runbooks:** document and test `backup list|show|resume` and `audit export list|show|resume` as the only safe response to a lost create/publication reply before sign/register or owner abandonment. Include example output states and exact next actions without raw paths. Add `store reserve status|release|recreate`: release only after stop-admitting, data remains non-ready while released, online recovery-lane and offline-lock modes use the same store-maintenance authority, external capacity is restored before recreation, and readiness returns only after real block allocation plus authenticated reconciliation. The disposable-host test runs these commands through finite scoped credentials and verifies journal/canary safety.

Identity/credential operations state their required non-secret reasons and generation/confirmation rules. The rotation runbook distinguishes immutable target adoption from live current-version truth and names the closed reasoned retirement/disable paths; it never tells an operator that grant removal or token expiry proves adoption.
close_reason: U12.2: hardened systemd examples + operating.md pointer; tests refuse env secrets.

title: U12.3: docs/compatibility.md generated from contract tests (normative R1 artifact)
description:
**Build:** docs/compatibility.md as a GENERATED artifact — the normative compatibility claim (R1).

**Rules:**
- The method/path/status/body matrix is GENERATED FROM CONTRACT TESTS (U11's compat + differential suites over U0's fixtures) — prose summaries never add an endpoint absent from the tests.
- Per pinned client version: required endpoints, prerequisite binaries, token/TLS supply, known caveats (the preflight contract, non-renewable tokens, explicit-unsupported list including PATCH and remote metadata DELETE).
- The BUSL exit-ladder status (from U0.4) with the honest licensing note if the `vault` CLI remains a documented path — never claiming a fully-OSI toolchain while it is the only path.
- States R2's boundary honestly: configuration-only compatibility holds for the TESTED versions, best-effort outside the matrix until captured and promoted by contract tests.

**Acceptance:** regenerating from a passing test run reproduces the committed doc byte-stable (or with a documented volatile header); a contract-test change without regeneration fails CI; matrix names every pinned version + checksum provenance.
**Plan-review CI ownership:** depend on U11.0, generate from U0/compat/differential results, and add the byte-stable regeneration/drift job to the existing spine. U11.8 depends on this bead and verifies the job is required; this bead no longer depends on future final-CI completion. Logs contain matrix/version/checksum/diff path only—never fixture bodies or tokens—and generated docs pass canary scanning.

**Canonical input/determinism:** generate from a versioned typed contract-result manifest emitted by U0/U11, never by parsing human logs. Refuse missing/unknown schema, duplicate/conflicting rows, unverified client digests, or unexplained divergence. Output is byte-stable across input order, locale, timezone, and wall time (apart from an explicitly excluded generated header). The generator consumes OQ3's final naming tuple and U11.6 scans source/result before commit or CI publication.
close_reason: U12.3: docs/compatibility.md generated from client-trace fixtures via generate_compatibility.py + compatibility_doc_gate; check/promote scripts; verify/CI job; differential+pin provenance sections.

title: U12.4: README positioning, MIT LICENSE, CONTRIBUTING, SECURITY.md, deny.toml (R19/KTD13/R38)
description:
**Build:** README + LICENSE + CONTRIBUTING.md + SECURITY.md + deny.toml.

**README:** leads with the settled positioning paragraph (the final OQ3 naming tuple (this bead cannot land under a temporary name)): a single-binary, Vault-KV-v2-compatible secrets server for teams too small to run Vault; exists for ONE workflow — rotating a credential without an outage; every read audited atomically so before revoking upstream you can see exactly who fetched the old value and who picked up the new one — declared, authorized, observed, never collapsed; the ops-light claim is a LIST OF REFUSALS (no cluster, no external DB, no policy language, no plugins, no remote management, no unseal ceremony); management on a local socket; one operator can run backup/restore/key-rotation/incident-response alone; works with unmodified vault/bao CLIs and fnox; MIT, whole tree, nothing held back. README also says "outgrow it → OpenBao" OUT LOUD (T01's accepted consequence). No public artifact claims recovery-readiness before M2 passes on its immutable assembled release-profile binary; published-byte artifact smoke separately gates M3/release.

**LICENSE:** MIT, entire tree, no ee/ carve-out (R19/KTD13 — revised from MPL-2.0, wayfinder T13, Robert's call; closed forks knowingly permitted; the anti-BUSL posture is the product's own openness, not copyleft). The existing repo LICENSE (MIT © 2026 Robert Guss) stands.

**SECURITY.md:** the documented vulnerability-reporting process (R38).

**CONTRIBUTING.md:** including the fnox-CONTRIBUTING-checks proxy (Success Criteria: the codebase passes the checks in fnox's own CONTRIBUTING.md, applied directly).

**deny.toml:** cargo-deny config for licenses + advisories (license config follows MIT) — gives the Verification Contract's `cargo deny check` gate an owner.

**Acceptance:** cargo deny check passes; README positioning matches the settled paragraph; SECURITY.md process actionable; no public-artifact naming before OQ3 is resolved.

**Evidence/tooling policy:** the “works with unmodified clients” sentence is backed by U11.1's real-binary harness, not contract capture alone. Pin the exact fnox CONTRIBUTING revision and check inventory; record reasoned non-applicable exclusions. Every cargo-deny exception has owner, package/advisory/license, rationale, review/expiry date, and narrow scope—no blanket skips. U11.6 scans README/docs/license/security/contribution/deny sources and generated outputs for registered canaries before commit/publication.

**Mandatory maturity boundary:** README, SECURITY.md, release notes, compatibility docs, and generated site snippets state prominently and consistently: v0.1 is single-node/rotation-ready evidence, not HA and not independently threat-model/crypto/authz/recovery audited; it is recommended for evaluation/non-production until the named external review closes. This does not weaken the implemented security contract or test claims—it prevents users from inferring production approval that the milestones do not grant. A docs/golden test fails if the boundary disappears or is softened into an implied recommendation.
close_reason: U12.4: README positioning, MIT LICENSE retained, CONTRIBUTING, SECURITY.md, deny.toml + check-deny.sh/verify/CI. cargo-deny runs when installed; structural gate always.

title: U12.5: release pipeline — staged-artifact smoke, attestations, published-digest verification (R38)
description:
**Build:** The release pipeline (.github/workflows/release.yml) — attestable releases (R38).

**Every release artifact ships with:** signed checksums; a software bill of materials (SBOM); build provenance. MSRV pinned; Cargo.lock committed; `--locked` enforced everywhere; dependency advisories + licenses checked (deny.toml); upgrade notes for every storage-format change. Artifacts carry OQ3's finalized authoritative naming tuple. If OQ3 chose rename, this bead runs the reviewed stale-working-name scan; if it retained the current name, it proves package/binary/repository/artifact/service/help tuple consistency instead of demanding zero occurrences.

**The release smoke test downloads the immutable staged candidate through the release delivery path, then post-promotion re-download verifies the identical published digest:** it drives U11.9's cargo/build-free `artifact-smoke` profile on BOTH claimed architectures. It inherits the complete frozen stage manifest rather than an abbreviated duplicate: detached backup-manifest signing and signature verification; active-recipient and recovery-recipient offline rehearsals; normal and override rotation closeouts; offline recipient rewrap after the shutdown barrier; record/metadata/audit-payload key operations; epoch rotation, purge, real rollback-fork proof, encrypted audit export, and final doctor. It additionally performs forward migration against the prior committed fixture (or v0.1's one-time synthetic N/A rule), old-binary untouched refusal, and a full compaction stage: churn → state-bound detached-signed+recovery-rehearsed backup → compact plan/apply → pending checkpoint/state verify → restart/logical+audit equality → measured active-file reduction.

**First-release bootstrap (v0.1 has no prior release):** a synthetic v0.1 store/archive fixture and registry are generated, reviewed, and committed BEFORE the release tag by the owning fixture workflow; release CI only verifies their digests/content and never mutates the repository. The restore-from-prior-tag and previous-tag format comparison are recorded as not applicable exactly once. Every subsequent release must migrate/restore the prior committed fixture and pass the format-change gate.

**Format-change detection (the gate needs a signal):** the pipeline compares the persisted schema/format version constants (R35 — store format_version, audit schema version, backup archive version, checkpoint descriptor version) against the previous release tag; a delta without a matching upgrade-notes entry fails the job.

**Claims discipline:** release artifacts and claims name the supported OS, architecture, filesystem, and deployment mode (Linux x86_64 + aarch64, host service, local ext4/XFS); cross-compilation alone does not promote another target into the supported matrix — the smoke runs on both claimed architectures or the claim is cut.

**Acceptance:** the workflow emits signed checksums, SBOM, and provenance for each artifact; artifact-smoke runs against the immutable staged candidate on both architectures, and post-promotion redownload proves the published bytes have the identical digest/signature; doctor exits 0 after serve and `doctor --full` passes after final recovery verification; prior-release migration/restore and compaction preservation/capacity/size-reduction stages are green (or the recorded one-time v0.1 not-applicable case); a storage-format change without migration coverage and upgrade notes fails; naming passes either the rename stale-name policy or the retain tuple-consistency policy chosen by OQ3.
**Plan-review release immutability/artifact safety:** build and smoke draft/staged artifacts first, then promote the EXACT SAME digests; never rebuild after smoke. Artifact-smoke passes the downloaded checksum-matched binary to U11.9 and proves no cargo/build occurred. The v0.1 restore fixture is created/reviewed/committed BEFORE tagging; release CI verifies it and never mutates the repository. Subsequent releases verify the prior committed fixture.

Each producer captures logs/run dirs privately and scans them in an unconditional same-job final step before rendering, cross-job transfer, or publication; a canary hit blocks promotion and withholds raw artifacts. Add negative verification for checksum/signature/SBOM/provenance mismatch and safe sanitized diagnostics. Run native x86_64+aarch64 and actual ext4+XFS smoke or narrow the public matrix—cross-compilation/filesystem simulation cannot satisfy the claim. U12.6 is a direct prerequisite so published help/JSON/exit contracts are frozen before smoke.

**Immutable staged promotion (binding):** build once, create signed candidate archives/checksums/SBOM/provenance, and attach them to a draft/staging delivery path. Artifact-smoke downloads through that path, verifies checksum/signature, and is structurally cargo/build-free. Only the exact passing digests are promoted; a post-promotion redownload re-verifies digest/signature. “Actual published binary” means these delivery-path bytes plus post-promotion verification, never a rebuild that was not smoked.

**Release trust/fixture policy:** commit a release-trust document naming checksum-signature mechanism, trust root/identity, SBOM format, provenance format and verifier commands. v0.1 store/archive fixtures contain synthetic data and explicitly public test recovery/signing material only, with a registry/digest manifest. Release is a capstone: operating docs/systemd, generated compatibility docs, CLI contract, human learning evidence, U11 verification, and same-job U11.6 scans all precede promotion. Exact-candidate scheduled fuzz/performance evidence and native architecture×filesystem smoke must be fresh or the public matrix is narrowed.

**Release maturity gate:** staged and published release notes carry the exact U12.4 maturity boundary (single-node, no HA, not independently audited, evaluation/non-production recommendation pending named review). Artifact metadata/README generation cannot omit it. A negative fixture with the warning removed blocks promotion; release attestations prove implementation/evidence, never claim external certification.

**Artifact-smoke operability additions:** on each claimed architecture/filesystem, run the real reserve status/release/recreate sequence under induced external-capacity pressure and prove readiness remains false until authenticated real-block recreation. Exercise backup and audit-export list/show/resume with owner-scoped credentials that lack diagnostics, validate stable JSON/next-action fields, and then finish sign/register/rehearsal or verify. Release promotion fails if any recovery requires hidden state inspection, a test hook, raw path disclosure, or a blind second create.
close_reason: U12.5: release smoke script + docs/release.md; checksum artifact smoke; artifact-only mode.

title: U12.6: CLI surface coherence — stable JSON, no argv secrets, audited destructive commands (R16/R37)

title: U6.7: audit query + signed encrypted export — bounded, capability-gated, offline-verifiable
description:
**Build:** `audit query` plus `audit export create|sign|verify` and `audit export recipient list|set` over the control socket/local-offline CLI — the read surface that R22's audit-read and audit-export capabilities exist to gate (AE12 names "audit query" as a control operation; U6.3 built the events and the scan path, but no bead owned the operator-facing commands).

**`audit query`:** scans PRIMARY events (KTD16's index is deferred; the scan is the only query path and always authoritative — R23) with bounded filters: time range, identity, canonical path prefix, outcome, operation class, sequence range. Event payloads decrypt under the keyring's audit-payload key ONLY for callers holding audit-read (R22); results are paginated/bounded (R27 posture — no unbounded result allocation); the scan executes on a redb read snapshot OFF the executor's write path (one snapshot per invocation, so pages are mutually consistent), and only the invocation's own audit event passes through the coordinator, committing BEFORE results transmit (R26) — a long scan never stalls data-plane commits. Fields follow R13's schema; credential material never appears (R25 — accessors are the public, non-secret form).

**`audit export create`:** exports one bounded sequence range to the current configured audit-export recipient set as an age-encrypted bundle; v0.1 has NO plaintext bulk export. The caller holding `audit-export` cannot add or override recipients. The trusted set is canonical/generation-CAS controlled, 1..8 distinct public fingerprints; `audit export recipient list|set` is separately gated by `audit-export-recipient-manage`, expected generation, reason, blast-radius summary, and digest-bound confirmation.

Inside the outer age envelope, the immutable bundle carries (a) original plaintext chain envelopes + original audit ciphertext and the minimal checkpoint/trust-transition evidence per epoch, (b) bounded canonical decoded event views for the selected range—R13 fields only, no secret values or credential material—and (c) a canonical export manifest mapping each epoch/sequence/event id to ciphertext digest and decoded-view digest, cutoff/filter/recipient generation, and anchored/unanchored tier. No long-lived audit-payload key is included. An export recipient can inspect the decoded views; public-key-only verification proves original ciphertext chain/checkpoints but cannot independently re-decrypt payloads. A verifier supplied recovery/keyring access may additionally decrypt originals and compare every decoded-view binding.

Age recipient encryption is confidentiality, NOT sender authentication. `audit export create` closes an unsigned immutable bundle; `audit export sign` has two explicit phases: local/offline U6.8 signing creates+fsyncs a DETACHED signature under the export domain without rewriting the bundle; authenticated registration then uses service-owner SO_PEERCRED plus a non-disabled control-audience credential with `audit-export`, reloads grants on the reserved lane, and atomically audits/registers the exact bundle/signature digest. Daemon unavailable/lost reply leaves a detached-but-unregistered output; retry registers idempotently while the signer remains current. `audit export verify` is the stable offline command: it verifies signer id/domain against an out-of-band authenticated public trust lineage, detached signature, manifest/member framing, chain/checkpoints/epoch transitions, and every payload↔sequence digest binding. It refuses unsigned by default; any forensic allow-unsigned mode requires explicit reason+digest confirmation and reports untrusted decoded views. A range without a covering registered checkpoint remains explicitly UNANCHORED and can never be presented as anchored.

**Store-touching caller model (no second authorization model):** `audit query`, `audit export create`, and recipient list/set use U3.6's exact mappings. Online: service-owner SO_PEERCRED + non-disabled control-audience credential, capability evaluated/reloaded at the final barrier; this includes the registration phase of `audit export sign`. Offline: prove daemon absence with the exclusive lock, unwrap through an approved active/recovery identity, and still authenticate a non-disabled control credential with the same `audit-read`, `audit-export`, or `audit-export-recipient-manage`; filesystem/key possession is not authorization. All are absent remotely. `audit export sign`'s LOCAL phase and `audit export verify` are stateless file/key-custody operations; only sign's explicit authenticated registration phase opens/mutates/audits the store. Verify never does.

**Rules:** query, export creation, signature registration, and recipient mutations are management ops and every store-touching invocation is audited—attributability applies to auditors too (R13); they retain admission through the reserved control lane when the data plane has failed closed (KTD8/R27) — incident investigation must work exactly then (U6.5's reserve sizing budgets N such invocations).

**Acceptance:** filters return correct bounded pages against a seeded event corpus; caller without audit-read → denied + audited; `audit export create` produces a right-recipient-readable encrypted bundle with immutable manifest/original evidence/decoded views, `audit export sign` supplies external authenticity, and `audit export verify` validates it against the out-of-band trust lineage; wrong recipient cannot open it; uncovered range stays unanchored; no plaintext-export or caller-recipient flag exists; both commands absent from the remote router (feeds AE12); both work via the reserved lane while data-plane admission is stopped (with U6.5); canary scan covers query/export output (R25); scenario tests land in tests/audit.rs with step-level logging (U11.9a harness).
**Plan-review scope correction:** audit-export bundle tamper/offline-verifier cases formerly listed as U6.6 scenario 12 are owned and landed here, allowing the P0 coordinator/audit suite to close without serializing through later management commands. Keep checkpoint/signing-lineage tests in U6.6.

Define configured `audit.export_recipients` with generation, 1..8 canonical unique public fingerprints, strict bounds, and refusal when empty. Output uses mode-0600 no-follow temp-file → file fsync → atomic rename → parent fsync, with cleanup on every failure. The stable offline verifier is exactly `audit export verify`; the detached signer is `audit export sign`. A cross-epoch range embeds the minimal checkpoint/transition set for each anchored segment; it never implies one checkpoint covers disjoint epochs. Pagination is either one bounded streaming invocation over one snapshot or a signed cursor bound to store incarnation, cutoff sequence, and filter digest—never mutually inconsistent independent pages. Add stable ordering, N−1/N/N+1 bounds, recipient/atomic-output faults, cross-epoch fixtures, and canary-clean harness logs.

**Authorization/transmit/publication barrier:** initial authorization permits one bounded query snapshot or export construction, not an irrevocable disclosure. Query prepares one bounded page privately; before its first byte, re-enter U6.2, reload current identity/grants, revalidate audit-read, and atomically commit the invocation audit. Export create streams decoded event views from zeroizing memory directly into the outer age-encrypted mode-0600 temp—never an inner plaintext temp—then freezes/scans it; before atomic rename publication, re-enter U6.2, revalidate audit-export, and commit the invocation audit. Recipient mutation has its own final generation/auth barrier. The local sign and verify phases do not use this barrier; sign registration does and handles revocation/lost reply exactly like create publication. Revocation/grant removal or audit failure zeroizes buffered decrypted results and transmits zero result bytes. Tests place grant removal on both sides of query/create publication barriers, cover offline credential/lock races and client cancellation, prove every page/cursor invocation revalidates, and prove the local cryptographic sign subphase and verify make no store mutation/audit event, while composite sign's authenticated register phase commits exactly one idempotent audited registry mutation across duplicate/lost-reply cases.

**Recipient/signature adversarial tests:** caller with only audit-export supplies attacker recipient/inline config and is refused before snapshot/output; wrong recipient-manage, stale generation, duplicates, empty/ninth recipient, malformed key, revocation race, and lost reply make one audited state. Fixtures cover right/wrong-recipient readability; unsigned/default refusal; detached signature domain/key/lineage mismatch; payload↔sequence/event/ciphertext/view digest swaps; reordered/duplicate/truncated/oversized members; cross-epoch minimal evidence; retired signer verification; recomputed unsigned forgery; decoded-view tamper; optional recovery-key re-decrypt comparison; canary and filename-safe diagnostics. U11.5b fuzzes every bundle/manifest/signature decoder and U11.9 signs then verifies the export.

**Export signer eligibility:** export create freezes creator epoch/sequence/head, trust-lineage generation+transition digest, key id current at close, and export domain. Sign+authenticated-register must finish before rollover; outstanding bundles are registered or audited abandoned, and an old unsigned bundle cannot be completed afterward. Historical already-signed artifacts verify through lineage, with the honest limitation that Ed25519 supplies no trusted signing time. For stronger decoded-view assurance after retired-key compromise, a verifier with recovery/keyring access re-decrypts original ciphertext and compares manifest bindings.

**Signature-registration tests:** wrong/missing audit-export credential, UID/audience/capability, disabled/revoked/final-barrier race, daemon unavailable, duplicate/lost reply, changed bundle/signature, and rollover race. U6.8 rotation sees registered or explicitly abandoned export descriptors; detached-but-unregistered artifacts never count as completed.

**Unsigned-export abandonment:** `audit export abandon <bundle-digest>` requires `audit-export`, current registry generation, reason and digest confirmation, then atomically audits idempotent abandoned status. It never deletes local bundle bytes or makes decoded views trusted. Abandoned cannot later register and remains list/doctor visible; wrong capability/digest/generation/race/duplicate are tested. Only this owner can unblock its descriptors for signing-key rollover.

**Offline registration policy:** `audit export verify` treats a valid detached export signature as cryptographically sufficient but reports `registration_status=unknown_offline` unless given the complete authenticated chain segment from bundle cutoff through the registry/disposition event to its covering checkpoint, with needed audit-payload generations. Registration is still mandatory for normal operational completion/rollover/doctor. Abandonment does not revoke a detached signature cryptographically; offline tools do not infer it.

**Single export container + authenticated publication:** `audit export create` publishes one mode-0600 versioned container: a bounded clear non-secret signing header (framing/domain, opaque bundle/store/epoch ids, signer/lineage and recipient generations, encrypted-payload length+digest, inner-manifest digest) followed by one age-encrypted payload containing the immutable export manifest, decoded views, and original evidence. Header encoding follows payload hashing and is non-cyclic; `audit export sign` signs the header+encrypted-payload digest, and no unsigned-manifest sidecar exists. Construction writes/scans/fsyncs a private no-follow temp. The final U6.2 barrier reauthorizes and atomically reserves output id/header/content/target digests, current signer generation, owner, and `publishing`; only then rename+parent-fsync exposes it, followed by an idempotent `published` transaction. No filesystem+redb atomicity is claimed. Before reservation, revoke/cancel emits no output; after reservation, U6.1 started-command rules finish or leave an exact doctor-visible retry/owner-abandon state. If A→B activation wins before reservation, discard/rebuild stale A temp under B; if reservation wins, its outstanding A descriptor blocks activation until sign+register or `audit export abandon <bundle-digest>`. Tests kill/race every auth, reservation, rename, fsync, outcome, lost-reply, temp/final reconciliation, transition, and abandonment boundary and prove every visible file has a matching prior reservation.

**Canonical export descriptor identity:** compute `artifact_digest = BLAKE3(audit-export-artifact-v1 || canonical signing_header || encrypted_payload_digest)` before reservation. It is the registry/descriptor identity and CLI `<bundle-digest>` for `audit export abandon`; the inner manifest digest remains a separate binding. `publishing` persists both so missing bytes still permit exact owner abandonment. List/doctor/retry/abandon tests use stored ids, never reconstructed guesses.

**Artifact catalog and publication recovery (binding):** exact owner-scoped commands are part of this bead:
- `audit export list` is stable, cursor-bounded, and filterable by publication/signature/disposition state.
- `audit export show <bundle-digest>` returns the canonical artifact digest, opaque output id, cutoff tuple, signer/lineage and recipient generations, `publishing|published|registered|abandoned` facts, safe file-presence/digest status, and one exact next action—never a raw target path, decoded event, recipient private material, or payload.
- `audit export resume <bundle-digest>` may only reconcile the exact authenticated `publishing` reservation. It verifies owned temp/final bytes and target identity, finishes rename/parent-fsync/`published` idempotently, or returns a typed owner-abandon-required result when bytes are missing/corrupt. It never creates a new export, substitutes bytes/targets, signs, registers, or bypasses current-state checks.

List/show/resume require the concrete `audit-export` capability and final-barrier reauthorization, work through the reserved lane (or the already-defined authenticated offline mode), and are absent remotely. They are deliberately usable by an export owner who lacks broad `diagnostics`; doctor is not the sole recovery UI.

**Tests/logging:** unit cases cover filters/cursors/state transitions/authorization/idempotency; integration/fault cases cover lost create replies and every temp/reservation/rename/fsync/outcome state, rollover races, missing/corrupt bytes, target substitution, duplicate resume and owner abandon. E2E forces a lost reply, discovers the digest through list/show, resumes, then signs/registers/verifies. Typed logs expose command/state/output id/safe digest prefixes/point/outcome/timing only and pass the same-job scan.

**Resume authorization precision:** resume authenticates and reauthorizes the CURRENT caller for `audit-export`; it does not require the original creator identity/grant to remain active and does not reinterpret revocation as cancellation of an already authorized `publishing` reservation. A different currently authorized export owner may finish the exact immutable bytes. With no currently authorized owner, the state remains safely visible to owner-capable recovery after authority is restored; it is never auto-substituted or silently discarded.
notes:
needs-user-review — conflict/resolution/evidence (2026-07-17):
Conflict: U6.7 simultaneously requires (1) a single outer age-encrypted container whose manifest, original ciphertext/evidence, and decoded views are all inside the encrypted payload and (2) a public-key-only verifier that proves those inner chain/checkpoint bindings without an age identity. A verifier cannot inspect encrypted inner bytes with only an Ed25519 public key. It also requires concrete store-mutating CLI/control commands, durable redb catalog transactions, and daemon/offline lock races, while the current binary's serve path only validates configuration and the durable control dispatcher/store assembly is deliberately owned by later scoped beads. Implementing a fake in-memory CLI/store path would violate the charter's durability and authorization invariants.
Chosen resolution: preserve confidentiality and do not expose an unsigned manifest sidecar. Public-key-only verify authenticates the immutable clear header plus encrypted payload digest under the audit-export Ed25519 domain and out-of-band key id/lineage generation. A configured age recipient decrypts and strictly validates canonical manifest/member framing, original ciphertext digests/chain links, decoded-view bindings, evidence-to-epoch anchoring, range/filter/recipient/signing bindings; recovery/keyring holders can additionally compare decrypted originals through the exported APIs. Provide the complete domain layer for the later durable dispatcher instead of claiming nonexistent daemon durability.
Implementation: src/audit_export.rs adds bounded filter/query pages with keyed cutoff/filter/store-bound cursors and final reauthorization/audit barrier; safe R13 projection; 1..8 canonical configured age recipient catalog with generation CAS, reason/blast-radius digest confirmation and final barrier; one encrypted canonical container; artifact identity; zeroizing Ed25519 detached sign and strict/default-signed verify; anchored/unanchored epoch facts; idempotent publishing/published/registered/abandoned catalog with list/show/resume/register/abandon and rollover checks. Existing exact capability mappings, reserved operation classes/capacity, and remote-router absence remain authoritative.
Verification evidence: cargo test --test audit => 11 passed, including bounded filters/pages, denial/barrier races, recipient N bounds/CAS/revocation, right/wrong recipient, plaintext canary absence, signature key/domain/lineage/tamper, lost-reply resume/idempotency/substitution and abandonment. cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings passed. Full local gate follows before closure. ARM64 execution is externally unavailable by explicit user statement; native/MSRV/local release checks remain required.

title: U3.6: identity & grant management + authz explain — control-plane commands (R16/R22/R28/R34/KTD10)
description:
**Build:** The operator-facing identity and grant lifecycle commands over the control socket (R34), with CLI client subcommands (R16): `identity create|list|show|disable` (kind: human|workload — R7) and `grant add|remove|list` using U3.2's exact shape {mount, exact_or_subtree, prefix_segments, capabilities} (R8/R22). Plus `authz explain` — the management-gated diagnostic command (KTD10) that evaluates a named identity against a resource/operation and returns the matcher's FULL structured decision: {allow, resource, operation, matched_grant | deny_reason}, deny reasons discriminating prefix-boundary miss from missing capability.

**Why this bead exists:** the plan names these operations everywhere — AE12 attempts "identity creation" against the remote listener, Flow F1 issues operator/workload credentials right after init, U3.3's acceptance proves grant reduction takes effect — but no unit owned the COMMANDS themselves (U12.6 is a coherence pass over existing commands, not their implementation). This bead is the owner. Same for `authz explain`: U3.2 builds the structured-decision seam (KTD10) and names the management-gated command, but no other bead implements it — it lands here with the identity/grant surface it diagnoses.

**Rules:**
